races.race_name: Look De Vega At Ballylinch Stud Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Skydance
runners.spotlight: Maiden tried at Group level on final four starts last season, showing smart level of form when second in Silver Flash Stakes here and final start when beaten under 4l in Group 1 Moyglare Stud Stakes at the Curragh; Irish 1,000 Guineas entry could well stay beyond 1m; big player.
runners.comment: Highly-tried maiden last season; Irish 1,000 Guineas entry could stay  1m+; big player

runners.name: Beatific
runners.spotlight: Frankel homebred half-sister to 7f winner Yuzu (RPR 74); dam German 7.5f AW/1m Listed winner (100), half-sister to German Listed winners Diatribe (6f 2yo/7f) and Daktani (1m); market best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Frankel filly for whom market best guide on debut

runners.name: Thundering On
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly, whose dam won the 1m2f Pretty Polly for this stable; both juvenile runs came at the Curragh in October on testing ground, on second occasion staying on well into a clear second in Group 3 Staffordstown Stud Stakes over this trip; will thrive over mid-distances in time but can make her mark at 1m; could prove the one to beat if handling this better ground.
runners.comment: Group 3 second last October; could prove the one to beat if handling this better ground

runners.name: Institute
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly out of a smart sprinting 2yo; twice runner-up in sprint maidens in October when racing prominently, at Naas and when keen at Dundalk in first-time tongue-tie, clear second to a long odds-on shot; upped in trip here and tongue-tie left off (Irish 1,000 Guineas entry).
runners.comment: Juvenile form at sprint trips but bred to appreciate this longer trip
runners.owner: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor

runners.name: Forty Six A
runners.spotlight: Some debut promise at the Curragh last month, much better 15 days later at Navan, running a bit in snatches, weakening late when second in 1m2f median auction maiden; back to 1m here in what is probably a stronger maiden so will need to improve again.
runners.comment: Navan second encouraging; back to 1m here in what appears a stronger maiden

runners.name: Cromac Quay
runners.spotlight: Night Of Thunder filly is closely related to Japanese 6f turf winner Irifi (inc 2yo); dam 1m 2yo/1m2f Listed winner (RPR 105), closely related to 1m Listed winner Mooharib, family of 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winners Rewilding and Dar Re Mi; another for market likely best guide on debut.
runners.comment: Night Of Thunder filly; another for market likely best guide on debut

runners.name: Elmakaya
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly; second foal of a 1m winner (RPR 90), a half-sister to winners Edelmira (1m4f Group 3) and Elishpour (useful 10.5f-2m), top family of Group 1 mid-distance/staying winners Ebadiyla, Enzeli and Estimate; Irish Oaks entry has to be worth a close look on debut but this trip could be on the short side; yard won this last year with a debutante.
runners.comment: Wootton Bassett filly and Irish Oaks entry; yard won this last year with a debutante
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Indomiknow
runners.spotlight: Invincible Spirit filly; okay efforts on both late-season juvenile runs at Dundalk without landing a blow; may want more time.
runners.comment: Okay efforts on both late-season juvenile runs at Dundalk; may want more time

runners.name: Rodeo Blues
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly cost 75,000euros as a yearling; first foal; dam maiden half-sister to winners Royal Julius (1m1f Group 2) and Hooflepuff (prolific 8.6f-1m4f AW), out of unraced half-sister to 1m2f 2yo Group 1 winner Goldamix; newcomer seemingly the lesser likely of Joseph O'Brien's pair but worth a look for future reference.
runners.comment: Seemingly the lesser likely of Joseph O'Brien's pair but worth a look for future reference
runners.owner: Annus Mirabilis Synd'/Sean O'Driscoll/Middle Cow Umlimited

runners.name: Justiciar
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly shaped well on debut on Derby weekend at the Curragh, staying on nicely from off the pace to finish third in 7f fillies maiden; disappointing favourite however in similar contest in August, fading tamely inside final 2f; should stay 1m; Irish 1,000 Guineas entry remains of interest.
runners.comment: Promising Curragh maiden debut no built on afterwards; should stay 1m

runners.name: Hiltons Pass
runners.spotlight: Twice soundly beaten in Curragh maidens last month at huge odds; easily passed over now.
runners.comment: Twice soundly beaten in Curragh maidens last month at huge odds; easily passed over

runners.name: Purely
runners.spotlight: Siyouni filly didn't count on Naas debut in October and later in month made plenty of use of when favourite at Dundalk but faded tamely over 7f; blinkers fitted for seasonal return and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Plenty of improvement needed on two juvenile runs; blinkers tried
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Gimme Energy
runners.spotlight: Blue Point filly ran okay on Naas debut in September but took a backward step on Dundalk AW two months later; probably best watched on return.
runners.comment: Major improvement needed on 2yo form to play a role on seasonal return

runners.name: Grey Intentions
runners.spotlight: Mildly encouraging run on second juvenile start but looks a more likely type for handicaps.
runners.comment: Mildly encouraging run on second juvenile start but looks a more likely type for handicaps
runners.owner: Thomas J Quinn & Brian P Whelan

runners.name: Sands Castle
runners.spotlight: Sands Of Mali filly soundly beaten in both late-season maidens at big odds; easily overlooked now.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both late-season maidens at big odds; easily overlooked now

runners.name: Lamberella
runners.spotlight: Gleneagles filly finished last of 16 on Cork debut in September; can only be watched for now.
runners.comment: Gleneagles filly finished last of 16 on Cork debut in September; can only be watched

races.race_name: Sunday 10th May Leopardstown Family Day Handicap

runners.name: Launch Time
runners.spotlight: Can be rated one of the leading contenders on his autumn turf form; third in two races over 1m2f at Navan; arguably better when fourth of 22 over this trip at the Curragh; cheekpieces may help.
runners.comment: Capable of making his presence felt based on late-season 2025 form, cheekpieces may help

runners.name: Romantic War
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after 21 outings; respectable run on final 2025 turf start; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, still a maiden after 21 outings, respectable run on final 2025 turf start

runners.name: Miss Americana
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability at a modest level last season; major chance if no ill-effects after a much-improved display at Gowran on Thursday; beaten only a short head and in line for a rise in the ratings.
runners.comment: Major chance if no ill-effects after a much-improved display at Gowran on Thursday

runners.name: Darwin's Angel
runners.spotlight: Twice finished fourth at a modest level last year; out of her depth in a maiden on return; this is more realistic but hard to fancy all the same.
runners.comment: Twice finished fourth at a modest level last year, out of her depth in a maiden on return

runners.name: Rampage
runners.spotlight: Four-time winner, including twice on turf early in his career; won at Dundalk during a busy 2025 campaign; ran respectably on the first of two Curragh outings last month; others preferred
runners.comment: Ran respectably on the first of two Curragh outings last month, others preferred
runners.owner: Mrs P McCourt/Whyte's Stamullen Syndicate/S Sweeney

runners.name: Heart Of Darkness
runners.spotlight: Sole win from 20 starts came in a 6f maiden on AW in 2024; 0-9 turf record is negative.
runners.comment: Uneven form at Dundalk during the winter, 0-9 turf record is negative
runners.owner: B O'Sullivan & D Whyte & D McHugh & M O'Loghlen

runners.name: Hale Bopp
runners.spotlight: Well below best on most recent Dundalk run; some sort of chance on the balance of winter AW form
runners.comment: Well below best on most recent Dundalk run, better judged on the balance of winter AW form

runners.name: Alto Sax
runners.spotlight: Two wins in claimers on turf in 2024; out of form at Dundalk until winning an apprentice handicap there 11 days ago; Nicola Burns was unable to claim on that occasion, and her valuable 5lb allowance is in play this time.
runners.comment: Out of form at Dundalk until winning there 11 days ago, won two claimers on turf in 2024

runners.name: Land Of The Giants
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has done most of his racing at Dundalk; career-low mark; still hard to fancy; stable has two other runners.
runners.comment: Maiden who has done most of his racing at Dundalk, career-low mark, still hard to fancy
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Nigel O'Hare & Philip Smith & Bart O'Sullivan

runners.name: Searcog
runners.spotlight: Won off lowly mark (42) at Punchestown last September; made the frame on her final two starts before being switched to hurdling; fair form in that sphere, apart from a poor handicap run last time; probably best over further; tongue-tie added now.
runners.comment: Fair hurdles form apart from a poor handicap run last time, probably best over further

runners.name: Cleopatra's Needle
runners.spotlight: Flat strike rate of 1-29 is a worry; however, her hurdles form shows that she has plenty of ability; won a competitive mares' handicap hurdle at the Galway festival on her final 2025 outing; hooded for the first time.
runners.comment: Flat strike rate of 1-29 is a worry, hurdles form shows that she has plenty of ability

runners.name: Sedro Woolley
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth in a decent Limerick maiden on debut last April (7f, soft); has qualified for a mark with two AW runs; one of the less exposed runners; worth considering.
runners.comment: Qualified for a mark with two AW runs, one of the less exposed runners, worth considering
runners.owner: South House Stud,Estate of Noel Murphy

runners.name: Chavajod
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent on turf last season; capable in this grade when on song but out of form at Dundalk during the winter campaign.
runners.comment: Capable in this grade when on song but out of form at Dundalk during the winter campaign
runners.owner: D P Coakley & J F Costello & A Connick & P Coakley

runners.name: Clifftop
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; had a run in a novice chase before competing from out of the handicap at Naas on return to the Flat; this is a more realistic task, but trainer may have a better chance with Cleopatra's Needle.
runners.comment: Out of the handicap at Naas on seasonal debut, this is a more realistic task
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Ralph Kelly & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Swift Charm
runners.spotlight: Weak AW form, including in a handicap in January; tongue-tie on; jockey booking is the only positive.
runners.comment: Weak AW form including in a handicap, tongue-tie on, jockey booking is the  only positive

runners.name: Bang Po
runners.spotlight: Heavy-ground winner in September 2023 but no joy since; has struggled in two Dundalk runs in recent months; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, only win was in 2023, has struggled in two Dundalk runs in recent months

runners.name: Comfort Line
runners.spotlight: Six-time Dundalk winner with his last win coming over C&D this time last year off 72; one of three runners for the stable; hard to be confident given his modest turf win record (3-70).
runners.comment: Has a better strike rate on AW, one of three runners for the stable, hard to be confident

runners.name: Believeth
runners.spotlight: Hinted at ability when midfield in a strong Curragh maiden on debut (6f) in August 2024; well beaten in two Dundalk runs this year; could be a dark horse on handicap debut
runners.comment: Ran once in 2024, two Dundalk runs this year, could be a dark horse on handicap debut

runners.name: Moonlight Molly
runners.spotlight: Out of her depth in maidens last year; no apparent chance but good 7lb claimer booked.
runners.comment: Out of her depth in maidens last year, no apparent chance but good 7lb claimer booked

runners.name: Bel Espoir
runners.spotlight: Finished in front of only a few rivals in maidens,; impossible to make a solid case for her.
runners.comment: Finished in front of only a few rivals in maidens, impossible to make a solid case for her
runners.owner: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill

runners.name: My Girl Robyn
runners.spotlight: Big price when in rear at Dundalk on handicap debut; stable's strong current form offers some hope; perhaps worth a look in the market.
runners.comment: Big price when in rear at Dundalk on handicap debut, stable's good form offers some hope

runners.name: Gobi Star
runners.spotlight: Very weak form (turf/AW) since last July; sole turf win was in a 5f maiden in 2022; not an obvious contender.
runners.comment: Very weak form (turf/AW) since last July, sole turf win was in a 5f maiden in 2022
runners.owner: Patrick J Digan & Ms Yvonne Hennessy

runners.name: Rock Etoile
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner; out of form at Dundalk during the winter; in rear at Gowran on Thursday; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, six-time winner, out of form at Dundalk, in rear at Gowran on Thursday
runners.owner: Conor McKenna & Patrick J McKenna & Emmet McKenna

runners.name: Faoladh
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a good spell of form on turf last summer, winning over C&D off 66; last month's Dundalk comeback run should have brought him on and worth considering despite high draw.
runners.comment: C&D winner last summer all the better for recent AW comeback run; considered

runners.name: Battle Borne
runners.spotlight: 10 of 13 starts have been at Dundalk, winning 7f maiden there in February; has struggled both starts since in handicaps and of limited appeal back on turf.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner has struggled since in handicaps; of limited appeal back on turf

runners.name: Merlin The Wizard
runners.spotlight: Three wins in Britain in 2023; regressive in last two seasons but best run for some time at Dundalk last month when second to an improver over today's trip under a positive ride, his second run for the yard; same mark here, could go well from a good draw.
runners.comment: Recent Dundalk second runs off same mark; could go well from a good draw

runners.name: Molto Amichi
runners.spotlight: Maiden placed several times last year including here over 1m1f (good) off 77; absent since a faller over hurdles in October and probably best watched on return.
runners.comment: Maiden ran well here last summer, faller over hurdles when last seen, best watched

runners.name: Walhaan
runners.spotlight: Seven Flat wins include here over 1m1f in 2024 and more recently on Dundalk AW in December (1m2f); mixed form since at same venue, 1m4f looking too far on latest; marked drop in trip here and visor replace cheekpieces; others preferred.
runners.comment: Marked drop in trip here and visor replace cheekpieces; others preferred

runners.name: Syzygy
runners.spotlight: Highly progressive from a low base last autumn, winning all three handicaps on heavy ground, at Gowran and twice at the Curragh; continued improvement on last month's return at latter venue when fine third of 25 from 7lb out of handicap in Irish Lincoln in first-time cheekpieces; encounters quicker conditions here, big player if handling them.
runners.comment: Lincoln third a big player if handling quicker ground

runners.name: Vega's Muse
runners.spotlight: Couple of near misses here last year before off the mark over this trip at Killarney in August (good); decent run at Bellewstown afterwards (8lb higher) on softer ground; one to consider.
runners.comment: Couple of near misses here last year before Killarney win; respected

runners.name: Beat The Devil
runners.spotlight: Busy AW campaign over the winter, winning over this trip and runner-up three times afterwards; ran well for a long way on latest but 1m4f seemed too far; back in trip with race-fitness guaranteed so definite each-way claims.
runners.comment: Fit from good AW campaign and back in trip; each-way claims

runners.name: Little Miss P
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; shaped well in Gowran and Galway maidens over this trip last summer on decent ground; opening mark looks okay so worth a look.
runners.comment: Promise in maidens last year; opening mark okay so worth a look

runners.name: Pallatine Hills
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well in a couple of handicaps last year off higher marks; usual blinkers left off for seasonal return; good draw and a contender if forward enough on seasonal return.
runners.comment: Maiden down to a decent mark and good draw; contender if forward enough on seasonal return

runners.name: Anvika
runners.spotlight: Her wins have been at 6f-7f but best run last year when runner-up in valuable C&D handicap on Champions Weekend in September off 1lb lower; heavy ground against her final start; one to consider although lacks a recent run.
runners.comment: C&D second in September gives her definite claims if forward enough on seasonal return

runners.name: Gotta Catch'em All
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden whose best form is over middle distances; ran well over this trip at Dundalk in October when last seen but may well need this comeback run.
runners.comment: Best form is over further; may need this comeback run
runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Spent All Me Money
runners.spotlight: Roscommon claimer winner for Jack Davison and winning start for current yard in 7f Naas handicap last August off 72 (good-to-yielding); ran no sort of race later in month at Roscommon however and absent since.
runners.comment: Naas winner last summer flopped at Roscommon; lacks a recent run

runners.name: Navagio
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner on yielding/soft for Johnny Murtagh; no success in Britain in recent years and poor form in three runs over hurdles for current yard over the winter; doesn't appeal as well treated.
runners.comment: Poor hurdles form over the winter; doesn't appeal as well treated

runners.name: Fixation
runners.spotlight: Made an immediate impact for new yard when scoring on stable debut at Dundalk (1m2f) in November and better again when second there over today's trip following month; likely still has more to offer on turf although wide draw far from ideal here.
runners.comment: Progressive on AW last autumn; likely more to offer on turf but wide draw far from ideal

runners.name: Va Va Vroom
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner here at 2 (good) but struggled in handicaps in first half of last season for Jessica Harrington and never counted on yard debut here in October on heavy; handicapper relenting but still on a high enough mark; wide draw a negative.
runners.comment: Struggled in handicaps last season; wide draw a negative on return

runners.name: Sonoran
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Tipperary maiden and then second in a Cork conditions race in first half of last season but didn't really progress; Dundalk run in November wasn't a bad effort but much more likely needed on return.
runners.comment: Didn't really progress as hoped last season; others preferred on return

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Three of seven wins have come at the Curragh, including in October off 66; heavy ground probably against him there final start; hasn't won off this high a mark so has it to do, and draw a negative.
runners.comment: 10yo hasn't won off this high a mark so has it to do, and draw a negative
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

races.race_name: Ballylinch Stud Red Rocks Stakes (Group 3) (Colts & Geldings)

runners.name: Power Blue
runners.spotlight: Won the first juvenile race of 2025; then third in a 5f Listed race; second to Albert Einstein in the 6f Marble Hill; fifth of 20 in the Coventry; well held in third behind True Love in the Railway Stakes; reversed form with the filly (finished slightly lame) to win the Phoenix Stakes under a positive ride; pedigree suggests he can stay 7f, ground should not be a problem.
runners.comment: Reversed previous form with True Love to win the Phoenix Stakes, big chance if he stays

runners.name: Dorset
runners.spotlight: Ran well in Group 2 Vintage Stakes at Goodwood; sacrificed in pacemaking role in the National Stakes (Curragh); beat 29 rivals to land the Goffs Million; followed that up with a front-running C&D Group 3 win in the Killavullan Stakes; talented apprentice is 2-2 on the Wootton Bassett colt who is effective on soft ground.
runners.comment: Goffs Million winner followed that up with C&D Group 3 win, strong contender, handles soft

runners.name: Flushing Meadows
runners.spotlight: Overcame inexperience to make a winning debut over 6f at the Curragh; did not look entirely straightforward (hung right) when beaten by a filly, Suzie Songs, in the Anglesey Stakes; doubts raised by 6l Group 3 defeat over C&D in July; however, Ryan Moore sticks with him in apparent preference to the higher-rated Dorset.
runners.comment: Promising sort, doubts raised by 6l Group 3 defeat over C&D in July, Ryan Moore aboard
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Geryon
runners.spotlight: Long-priced Curragh maiden winner on debut last August; subsequently form ties in with smart Ballydoyle colts Constitution River and Hawk Mountain; Beresford Stakes second was advertised by Hawk Mountain in winning the William Hill Futurity; tongue-tie on now.
runners.comment: Form ties in with smart Ballydoyle colts Constitution River and Hawk Mountain, respected

runners.name: Thesecretadversary
runners.spotlight: Chesham Stakes runner-up; not disgraced in a Grade 1 in Canada in September after beating good yardstick Brussels in a Listed race at Tipperary; easy ground is a concern based on his failure to land short odds at Ayr in July.
runners.comment: Not disgraced in a Grade 1 in Canada after beating good yardstick Brussels at Tipperary
runners.owner: Cayton Park Stud & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Mumhan
runners.spotlight: Debut maiden winner over 6f at the Curragh; beaten 14l when last of five behind Dorset in Killavullan Stakes over C&D.
runners.comment: Debut maiden winner, beaten 14l when last of five in Killavullan Stakes over C&D

runners.name: Redemption Road
runners.spotlight: 89-rated gelding faces a daunting task in a race of this standard, fit from a Dundalk run.
runners.comment: 89-rated gelding faces a daunting task in a race of this standard, fit from a Dundalk run

runners.name: Iron Lily
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; no match for a Ballydoyle favourite at the Curragh (7f, soft to heavy) last October; had plenty in reserve when winning a Dundalk later that month; can win more races this term; this may prove too demanding on return.
runners.comment: Had plenty in reserve when winning a Dundalk last October, can win more races this term

runners.name: The Publican's Son
runners.spotlight: Belied his 40-1 odds with a strong-finishing second in Group 3 contest at the Curragh (6f, good to yielding) where he was the sole newcomer in a field of ten; relative inexperience may have been a factor when he finished in rear in the Middle Park.
runners.comment: In rear in the Middle Park after a promising debut in a Group 3 event at the Curragh

runners.name: Trojan Warrior
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt out of a Fastnet Rock mare; left an impression he would stay a lot further when winning over 6f at the Curragh on only start at two; promising.
runners.comment: Left an impression he would stay a lot further when winning over 6f on only start at two
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Warsaw
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut over 5f at Navan last June; not seen again after a mid-division finish in the Coventry; tongue-tie fitted; beat watched.
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Navan, not seen again after a mid-division finish in the Coventry

runners.name: True Love
runners.spotlight: Backed up Queen Mary win when much too good for the colts in the Railway Stakes; reported slightly lame after odds-on Phoenix Stakes defeat; quickly back in the groove when beating Havana Anna in the Cheveley Park; started slowly and never posed a serious threat over 5f in the Breeders' Cup Juvenile Turf Sprint; steps up to 7f, clear pick here if coping with trip/easy ground.
runners.comment: Queen Mary/Cheveley Park winner, steps up to 7f, clear best if coping with trip/ground
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Composing
runners.spotlight: Summer hat-trick culminated in Group 2 win at the Curragh, beating Suzie Songs (7f, good); beaten favourite in the Moyglare and Goffs Million at the same venue; unplaced in the Fillies' Mile on final start; officially rated 10lb below stablemate True Love.
runners.comment: Very experienced filly, solid form over this trip, rated 10lb below stablemate True Love

runners.name: Black Caviar Gold
runners.spotlight: Was well behind Pivotal Attack when third on debut at Galway (7f, yielding); hugely improved effort on third start when easily winning a 7f maiden at Cork on soft; promoted from 85 to 108 for Curragh Group 3 win (7f soft) that involved a reversal of form with Pivotal Attack; needs to sustain that improvement.
runners.comment: Promoted from 85 to 108 for Curragh Group 3 win on soft, needs to sustain that improvement

runners.name: Suzie Songs
runners.spotlight: Caused a surprise when getting the better of hot favourite Flushing Meadows in the Anglesey Stakes; raced a bit too keenly and failed to cope with Composing in Debutante Stakes and didn't show her true form when last of seven in the Group 1 Moyglare; hooded now and not without a chance
runners.comment: Group 3 winner and Group 2 runner-up, finished last in the Moyglare, can hold her own here

runners.name: Kensington Lane
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fifth attempt in fine style at Gowran, confirming the promise she showed when chasing home the very useful Geryon at the Curragh; flopped in Group 3 race won by Black Caviar Gold on final start.
runners.comment: Experienced filly, flopped in Group 3 race won by Black Caviar Gold on final start at two

runners.name: Blanc De Blanc
runners.spotlight: Still looked raw when recording the second of two Dundalk 1m wins; interesting prospect but may find 7f too sharp; might not want too much ease in the ground.
runners.comment: Still looked raw when recording the second of two Dundalk 1m wins, may find 7f too sharp

runners.name: Caught U Sleeping
runners.spotlight: Overcame inexperience when making a winning debut in a 7f fillies' maiden at Naas last August; kept on for third behind the subsequent Prix Marcel Boussac winner Diamond Necklace in a C&D Listed race; ran poorly over 1m on heavy on final start; hooded now.
runners.comment: Ran poorly over 1m on heavy on final start, better judged on Listed third over C&D

runners.name: Slaney View
runners.spotlight: Placed on all three starts; capable of winning races; 77-rated filly has an unenviable task in this grade
runners.comment: Placed on all three starts, capable of winning races, has an unenviable task in this grade
runners.owner: Molly & Paul Willis Racing, Mrs J S Bolger

runners.name: Magny Cours
runners.spotlight: Won on debut at Listowel (6f, good to yielding); good form in defeat, notably when second in a 7f Group 3 event at Chantilly on final start; well held by Black Caviar Gold on Curragh running.
runners.comment: Group 3 second in France last October, well held by Black Caviar Gold on Curragh running

runners.name: Anushka
runners.spotlight: Dundalk maiden winner on her third and final start last season; this is highly ambitious for an 82-rated filly.
runners.comment: Dundalk maiden winner on her third start, this is highly ambitious for an 82-rated filly

runners.name: Pivotal Attack
runners.spotlight: Promise over 6f on debut at Fairyhouse and then bolted up over 7f at Galway (yielding); not disgraced in the Moyglare when racing keenly; ended the season with a positive effort behind Black Caviar Gold in a Curragh Group 3; stable's Cercene was third here last year as a prelude to better things.
runners.comment: Ended last season with a positive effort behind Black Caviar Gold in a Curragh Group 3

runners.name: River Ara
runners.spotlight: No match for Black Caviar Gold in a maiden at Cork; course 1m nursery winner on her final start; stable has stronger candidates.
runners.comment: No match for Black Caviar Gold in a maiden, nursery winner, stable has stronger candidates
runners.owner: Westerberg & Mrs Magnier & M Tabor & D Smith

runners.name: Mayflower
runners.spotlight: Handled soft ground when making a winning debut at this venue last July; should be able to pay her way this season, but this represents a big jump in class.
runners.comment: Handled soft ground when making a winning debut at this venue last July, big jump in class

runners.name: Killashee Warrior
runners.spotlight: Thurles maiden winner after a fine effort in a C&D Listed race (1 1.2l behind third-placed Caught U Sleeping); needs to raise her game to be competitive here.
runners.comment: Thurles maiden winner after a fine effort in a C&D Listed race, needs to raise her game

runners.name: White Lily
runners.spotlight: Toward rear behind Black Caviar Gold on debut; long-priced third over 6f at Naas on second start; looks out of her depth at this level
runners.comment: Long-priced third over 6f at Naas last autumn, looks out of her depth at this level

runners.name: A Boy Named Susie
runners.spotlight: Debut maiden winner; lost out by only a short head to Christmas Day in the 1m1f Eyrefield Stakes here in October on his fourth start; built on that when 2l second to Pierre Bonnard at Saint-Cloud; does not need to improve much to win a Pattern race; solid chance here.
runners.comment: Needs to reverse form with Pierre Bonnard and Christmas Day to win, solid place chance

runners.name: Pierre Bonnard
runners.spotlight: Made rapid progress to become a top middle-distance prospect after winning a 1m Dundalk maiden; asserted strongly when beating stablemate Endorsement in the Zetland Stakes (1m2f, good to firm); continued his rise with a smart display to beat A Boy Names Susie on very soft ground in the Criterium de Saint-Cloud; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Group 1 winner at Saint-Cloud, rematch with 2l second A Boy Named Susie on 5lb worse terms

runners.name: Italy
runners.spotlight: Split the ill-fated Gewan and Distant Storm in the Acomb at York; then ran well behind Zavateri and stablemate Gstaad in National Stakes at the Curragh; repeated a hard-pulling tendency in the Dewhurst; needs to settle better to do full justice to his ability; hooded now for seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Smart form at two in Britain and when third in the National Stakes, needs to settle better

runners.name: Endorsement
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his previous form when second to stablemate Pierre Bonnard in the Zetland last time; well held on his running at Saint-Cloud, dropping away in the final furlong.
runners.comment: Good effort behind Pierre Bonnard in the Zetland Stakes, well held on Saint-Cloud running

runners.name: Victory Tip
runners.spotlight: Coped admirably with heavy ground on debut at the Curragh; faces a very stiff task at this level with such limited experience.
runners.comment: Coped admirably with heavy ground on debut at the Curragh, stiff task at this level

runners.name: Christmas Day
runners.spotlight: Short head winner from A Boy Named Susie in the Eyrefield Stakes over 1m1f here last October; extra furlong should suit but would probably need more than a 2lb concession to account for Pierre Bonnard.
runners.comment: Short head winner from A Boy Named Susie over 1m1f here, extra furlong should suit

runners.name: Shosholoza
runners.spotlight: Created a strong impression when beating a subsequent Curragh winner by a wide margin  on heavy ground at Tipperary on his debut; creditable fifth at Saint-Cloud only three weeks later; held by Pierre Bonnard and A Boy Named Susie but open to improvement.
runners.comment: Creditable fifth at Saint-Cloud but held by Pierre Bonnard and A Boy Named Susie
runners.owner: Rectory Road Hldgs Ltd/E Palmer/W Slattery/Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: South Island
runners.spotlight: Curragh maiden winner; unplaced in races won by Endorsement and Christmas Day; trainer's second string.
runners.comment: Unplaced in races won by Endorsement and Christmas Day, trainer's second string

runners.name: James J Braddock
runners.spotlight: Promising Navan debut when strong finishing 1/2l second over 1m, despite rider losing an iron close home (yielding); impressed in beating two stablemates in testing conditions in a 1m Curragh maiden at the beginning of November; should make the grade at Pattern level
runners.comment: Impressed in winning a 1m Curragh maiden on heavy gorund last November, promising colt
runners.owner: Aziz Kheir Aust Partners & Kevin Blake

runners.name: Another Day Done
runners.spotlight: Nicely improved in first-time visor to handsomely win 1m Dundalk handicap in January off 63 (beating a subsequent winner); not clear run and hung right when only fourth over same C&D 10 days later off 12lb higher mark; cheekpieces now replace visor and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Dundalk winner now upped in trip and one to consider

runners.name: Madbadanddangerous
runners.spotlight: Decent level of form in three outings in second half of last season, notably getting up late in 7.5f Naas auction event and slightly shorter trip against him at same venue final start when staying on third in valuable auction series final; good chance he'll improve for this step up in trip and on an okay mark.
runners.comment: Juvenile form suggested he would appreciate this step up in trip; okay mark so respected

runners.name: Cherry Hill Girl
runners.spotlight: Comfortable winner of a Listowel maiden (1m, heavy) before respectable run in C&D handicap (soft); didn't count over shorter trip on last month's seasonal return at Naas, now takes a step up in trip (now really bred for it).
runners.comment: 1m winner last year but not really bred for this longer trip

runners.name: Cape Cod
runners.spotlight: Progressive juvenile form, placed in maidens at Punchestown and the Curragh before off the mark final start over 1m at Naas in October, making all; should stay 1m2f; type to continue improving and yard have a good recent record in this.
runners.comment: Steadily progressive at two; should stay 1m2f; type to continue improving

runners.name: So Must I
runners.spotlight: Stepped up nicely on previous form to beat a stablemate and favourite (pair clear) in Dundalk rated race over slightly further than this in February; type to keep improving at this trip and rider/trainer having a good time of it of late.
runners.comment: Dundalk winner over this trip one to keep improving; rider/trainer having good time of it

runners.name: Extravagant
runners.spotlight: Solid maiden runs first two starts at two and off the mark on handicap debut off 80 at Killarney in August (1m, good); didn't seem to get home in heavy ground at Listowel following month but remains open to progress back on better ground and half-brother to high-class Cloth Of Stars should appreciate this trip.
runners.comment: Remains open to progress back on better ground and should appreciate this longer trip

runners.name: Marjorie Daw
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced at two, stepping up nicely from Listowel debut to land Gowran fillies maiden over an extended mile in October, needing every yard to get back up close home (first two clear) on heavy; will be well suited by 1m2f; quicker conditions here and high enough initial mark, although open to more improvement than most
runners.comment: Gowran maiden winner will relish 1m2f; high enough mark but unexposed
runners.owner: T O'Sullivan & S R Stafford & Crampscastle Bloodstock Ltd

runners.name: Pierre Grosse
runners.spotlight: Naas auction series final sixth was an improvement on his maiden runs before that; not too far off Madbandandangerous on that running on these revised terms and longer trip should suit.
runners.comment: Not far off Madbandandangerous on Naas run on these revised terms; longer trip to suit

races.race_name: Sky Bet Club British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Adonius
runners.spotlight: April foal by Soldier's Call; sent off 12-1 when bolting up by 7l over C&D (soft; RPR 84+) eight days ago; has a 5lb penalty to carry and conditions may not be quite so testing this time, but that still provides a daunting level for the others to aim at.
runners.comment: Bolted up by 7l on his debut over C&D eight days ago; sets a daunting level

runners.name: Penny Arcade
runners.spotlight: February foal by Ardad; 30,000gns yearling; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 73), half-sister to winners Absolutely So (7f Group 3), Weekend Getaway (7.5f-8.7f) and Forbidden Land (7f), is granddaughter of 1m2f Group 2 winner; stable has a fine record with 2yo newcomers and any market support for her would be significant.
runners.comment: Stable has a fine record with 2yo newcomers; market support for her would be significant

runners.name: Ponte Carlo
runners.spotlight: April foal by Soldier's Call; 6,500gns foal; half-brother to winners Claim The Stars (4.7f-7.5f including AW/France/Germany/Belgium; RPR 90), Fraser Island (1m2f Flat, 85; 2m hurdle), National Glory (6f; 82), and Pickersgill (6f; 73); dam 5f 2yo winner (including Group 3; 104); stable has few winning 2yo newcomers and he has a useful standard to aim at in any case.
runners.comment: Stable has few winning 2yo newcomers and he has a useful standard to aim at

runners.name: Rlasthope
runners.spotlight: February foal by Rajasinghe; sent off 10-1 and showed promise when fifth of the 15 runners in the Brocklesby at Doncaster (5f, good to soft) 15 days ago; open to improvement, but it will be needed.
runners.comment: Showed promise when fifth in the Brocklesby 15 days ago; improvement will be needed

runners.name: Thestral
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on his second start in a Newcastle novice (7f, AW) in December, but having been gelded he finished well behind Blues And Royals and Just A Gambler on handicap debut at Kempton (1m, AW) 18 days ago; blinkers worn there quickly removed; others preferred on turf debut.
runners.comment: Well behind two of these on handicap debut at Kempton 18 days ago; others preferred

runners.name: Blues And Royals
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive and having been gelded he did it nicely when making a successful handicap/seasonal debut at Kempton (1m, AW) last month; 3lb rise doesn't look harsh now returned to turf and high on list from the rails draw.
runners.comment: Did it nicely when making a successful handicap/seasonal debut at Kempton last month
runners.owner: D Fish, S Jones & J Barnett

runners.name: Saxon Prince
runners.spotlight: All five starts for Clive Cox on the AW, beaten a short head in a four-runner handicap at Kempton (7f) last month; races off the same mark for this turf/stable debut having since changed hands for 24,000gns, but the winner didn't do much for the form next time; drawn widest.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head at Kempton last time, but winner didn't frank the form; drawn widest
runners.owner: A Scaife, C Scaife, G Scaife

runners.name: Parole Officer
runners.spotlight: Consistent rather than progressive in eight starts last year, the latest when fourth of seven on stable debut at Newcastle (1m, AW) in November; more will be needed back from five months off.
runners.comment: Consistent rather than progressive last year; more needed back from five months off

runners.name: Just A Gambler
runners.spotlight: Made a successful handicap/seasonal debut at Chelmsford (1m, AW) last month and ran just as well when beaten just over a length into third of nine behind Blues And Royals at Kempton (1m, AW) 13 days later; 3lb better off, but his rival was making his reappearance; cheekpieces given a go.
runners.comment: Finished just over a length behind Blues And Royals at Kempton last time; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Kameko Fever
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive in five starts on the AW during the winter, going down by a neck at Newcastle (1m; first start since being gelded) last month; just 1lb higher, but the winner finished tailed off at Nottingham on Wednesday; has a bit to prove on turf debut.
runners.comment: Beaten a neck at Newcastle last month, but form not franked; a bit to prove on turf debut
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Mayaada
runners.spotlight: Consistent prior to winning a four-runner Epsom maiden (7f, good) last August, but held since; finished tailed off on her Southwell return (7f, AW) 15 days ago; drawn wide; look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Tailed off on her Southwell return 15 days ago; drawn wide; look elsewhere

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: Best effort in four starts for Harry Eustace as a 2yo when runner-up in a Brighton maiden (7f, soft) in September, but has regressed badly since (including in three outings for this yard) and virtually ran out at Wolverhampton last time.
runners.comment: Finished runner-up in a Brighton maiden last September, but has regressed badly since

runners.name: Keep An Eye On It
runners.spotlight: Didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages when beaten a short head on handicap debut at Southwell (7f, AW) ten days ago; only 1lb higher and stepping up another furlong may suit him even better, with the return to turf unlikely to be a problem; major player.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head at Southwell last time; extra furlong should suit; major player

runners.name: Ibn Altheeb
runners.spotlight: Sole win for David Loughnane came in a Wolverhampton nursery (8.6f, AW; first-time cheekpieces) in October; picked up for 20,000gns later that month and now makes his stable debut after 174 days off; well drawn; watch market.
runners.comment: Wolverhampton AW winner last year; makes his stable debut after 174 days off; watch market

runners.name: Supreme Clarets
runners.spotlight: Mainly consistent in six starts as a 2yo without appearing to progress, going closest when beaten a head in a Salisbury seller (7f, heavy) in September; returns from six months off and will need to step up, especially from his wide draw; stable also runs Kameko Fever.
runners.comment: Mainly consistent in six starts as a 2yo without progressing; off six months; drawn wide

runners.name: Alkumatic Jo Jo
runners.spotlight: In the frame in all four starts over 7f/1m on AW/soft last year; return to this trip should suit and the market should reveal what is expected after five months off.
runners.comment: In the frame in all four starts last year; market should reveal what is expected on return

runners.name: Suddenly I See
runners.spotlight: Both runs last year came in maidens here (7f, good), winning gamely by half a length on the second occasion; extra furlong shouldn't be a problem now making his handicap debut after seven months off and the market should be revealing; stable also runs Just A Gambler.
runners.comment: Third and first over 7f here last year; market useful on return/handicap debut

races.race_name: Sky Bet Build A Bet Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Perfect Part
runners.spotlight: 0-13 since her successful racecourse debut albeit highly tried on occasions, but she made a fine return when fourth of 22 in the Spring Mile at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) 15 days ago and that form has already received a boost; well drawn and a big player from the same mark; stable also runs Callianassa.
runners.comment: Fine return when fourth of 22 in the Spring Mile 15 days ago; big player from same mark

runners.name: Epidavros
runners.spotlight: Both wins (7.7f/1m1f) in September 2024, but 0-13 since; now back off her last winning mark, but she may just need this after 194 days off.
runners.comment: Back off her last winning mark, but she may just need this after 194 days off

runners.name: Dunkeld Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Record in nurseries last year read 1311222, the wins coming over 7f-8.5f; still 13lb higher than when last successful, though, so will need to hit the ground running in order to win this after 162 days off.
runners.comment: Record in nurseries last year read 1311222; still 13lb higher than when last successful

runners.name: Second Fiddle
runners.spotlight: Made it 15th-time lucky on stable debut at Hamilton (1m, soft) last September and then won five of his next six starts on the AW, but held in both outings at the start of the year; bit to prove back on turf after three months off.
runners.comment: Won six times between September and December last year, but quiet in last two starts

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: All four wins have come over 7f/1m on the AW, the latest in February; return to this trip should suit, but she is 0-9 on turf and a few of the others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: Return to this trip should suit, but he is 0-9 on turf; others make greater appeal

runners.name: Jannas Journey
runners.spotlight: Enjoyed a fine time of it between July and September last year, winning seven times including over 7f here; may have needed her reappearance when well behind Bellarchi back here (7f) eight days ago, but she will need to step forward and is drawn wide; stable also runs Geo.
runners.comment: Seven wins between July and September last year; well behind Bellarchi on recent return

runners.name: Ice Sovereigns
runners.spotlight: Won a novice and a nursery at Carlisle (7f, good) last year, but was completely out of her depth when last of ten (behind Samra Green) in the Group 2 Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) on her final outing; this is much more realistic on her return and worth considering, especially if backed.
runners.comment: Dual Carlisle winner last year but outclassed in the Rockfel; this is more realistic

runners.name: Asteverdi
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced last year and ended the season quietly, but she is 2lb below her last winning mark and, although off six months, she can go well fresh; well worth a market check now starting out for another new yard.
runners.comment: 2lb below last winning mark and can go well fresh; worth a market check on stable debut

runners.name: Geo
runners.spotlight: 1-11 for Eve Johnson Houghton, the win coming on nursery debut at Newbury (7f, good to firm) in August 2024; possibly unlucky when fourth of 11 at Southwell (1m, AW) in October; changed hands for 9,000gns soon afterwards; market informative on stable debut after six months off.
runners.comment: 1-11 for Eve Johnson Houghton; market informative on stable debut after six months off

runners.name: Samra Green
runners.spotlight: Did it well when making a successful debut at Haydock (7f, good) last August, but found the Group 2 Rockfel at Newmarket (7f, good to firm) rather tougher the following month, not that she was disgraced in finishing seventh of 11; market should be revealing on return and she still holds an Irish 1,000 Guineas entry; stable also runs Dunkeld Dreamer.
runners.comment: Finished seventh in the Rockfel last September; still holds an Irish 1,000 Guineas entry

runners.name: Bellarchi
runners.spotlight: 3-4 over 7f here, the latest win coming on good to soft ground eight days ago; has won over this trip elsewhere and high on the list from 3lb higher.
runners.comment: 3-4 over 7f here, the latest win eight days ago; has won over 1m elswhere; high on list

races.race_name: Sky Bet For The Fans Holyrood Handicap

runners.name: Thunder Wonder
runners.spotlight: Efforts since last summer leave plenty to be desired, including last month's Wolverhampton reappearance (8.6f, AW; first start since being gelded); however, he is 2-2 here (1m/1m1f) and 7lb lower than when last successful; worth a second look; stable also runs Sir Paul Ramsey.
runners.comment: Modest since last summer, but he is 2-2 around here and 7lb below his last winning mark

runners.name: Flying Fletcher
runners.spotlight: Mainly seen on the Newcastle AW (3-18) and ran well to finish third of 12 off this mark there (1m) 16 days ago, but he is 0-10 on turf; others preferred.
runners.comment: Mainly seen on the Newcastle AW (3-18) and is 0-10 on turf; others preferred

runners.name: Sea Force
runners.spotlight: Won a Nottingham maiden (1m, good) and a Yarmouth handicap (1m1f, soft) last year; now 6lb higher and returns from seven months off, but he was only beaten a neck on last season's reappearance and there may well be even more to come from him this year; respected.
runners.comment: Returns from seven months off, but was only beaten a neck on last season's reappearance

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than for the latest of two wins on turf last year and suggested a stiffer test might suit when beaten just over a length into fourth of 12 at Lingfield (1m, AW) nine days ago; shortlisted.
runners.comment: 1lb above last winning mark and latest effort suggested this stiffer test might suit

runners.name: Dwindling Funds
runners.spotlight: Only made her debut a year ago and won six times over 7f-1m2f on turf between July and October, five of them at Ayr; ended the campaign with a nose defeat here (1m, soft) and just 1lb higher, but not sure what to expect after five months off; 2lb out of the weights.
runners.comment: Won six times over 7f-1m2f on turf between July and October last year; off five months
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Per Contra
runners.spotlight: Won three of his first four starts for Ollie Sangster between July 2023 and April 2024, but has only been successful in one of his last 17 starts and that was on the AW; needs to step up from his Doncaster return (1m2f, good to soft) a fortnight ago.
runners.comment: Successful in one of his last 17 starts; needs to step up from his Doncaster return

runners.name: Noche Clasica
runners.spotlight: Progressive in six starts for three different stables last year (641131) including making a successful debut for this one at Redcar (1m, good to soft) in October; has a 6lb higher mark and a 167-day absence to overcome, but she remains open to further improvement.
runners.comment: Progressive in six starts last year (641131); off 167 days but open to further improvement

runners.name: Impartiality
runners.spotlight: Won his first three starts in 2025 so the 195-day absence isn't a major concern, but he is still 12lb above his last winning mark; cheekpieces worn in last four outings removed.
runners.comment: Has won fresh so 195-day absence not a major concern; still 12lb above last winning mark

runners.name: Saint Etienne
runners.spotlight: Four wins over 9.5f/1m2f on AW/good to soft in France, but he finished well held on stable debut in the Rosebery at Kempton (1m3f, AW) 15 days ago; may have needed the run after ten months off and the usual tongue-tie goes back on, but he still has enough to prove.
runners.comment: Four wins in France; may have needed the run at Kempton last time, but has enough to prove
runners.owner: The Cat And The Mag Partnership

runners.name: James McHenry
runners.spotlight: Three wins at about 1m and finished runner-up in the 2024 Cambridgeshire, but lightly raced since; not beaten far into fourth of ten at Kempton (1m, AW) last time, but still 5lb above his last winning mark and doesn't look solid back from another short break; stable also runs Noche Clasica.
runners.comment: Lightly raced since finishing runner-up in the 2024 Cambridgeshire; doesn't look solid

runners.name: Mirsky
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (1m, good to firm) in May and Epsom (8.5f, good to soft) last year, but well held in his last two starts; his record suggests this first start in 281 days may be needed.
runners.comment: Won twice last year, but quiet in last two starts; record suggests the run may be needed

runners.name: Redarna
runners.spotlight: Showed he still had it what it takes when gaining his ninth course success at Ayr (1m, good to soft) last September and ran well despite a 10lb rise back there 12 days later; has tended to need his reappearance in recent years.
runners.comment: 12yo who has won 17 races; has tended to need his reappearance in recent years

races.race_name: Sky Bet Extra Places Handicap (GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Say What You See
runners.spotlight: Last five races in 2025 (1m2f/1m4f) saw him first past the post three times and second twice; on final start he finished well on top at Doncaster (1m2f) but was disqualified for a banned substance; only 2lb higher today and made a respectable return to action at Doncaster two weeks ago; return to 1m4f should be okay and he's one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Excellent end to 2025 and a respectable return to action at Doncaster two weeks ago

runners.name: Dust Cover
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced before finding his stride upped in trip on AW this winter, winning three of his last four starts (Southwell/Kempton) and beaten a nose in the other; all those were 1m3f but the style of them, launching late bids, gives clear scope for further improvement over this extra 1f if he's as effective on turf.
runners.comment: AW success story at 1m3f; clear scope for more over 1m4f if he's as effective on turf

runners.name: Made All
runners.spotlight: Comes from off the pace; twice beaten about 2l over C&D last July; flourished elsewhere with three 1m4f turf wins last May-August and he returned from hurdling to break his AW duck at Wolverhampton last month; up 3lb today; major player if he's effective at this track.
runners.comment: Did well on turf last year; AW win on return; major player if he's effective at this track

runners.name: L'Eagle Aid
runners.spotlight: 238 days off before his Newcastle run three weeks ago when he may well have been in need of the race and interference exacerbated his margin of defeat; possibilities if that has brought him back to his best.
runners.comment: Possibilities if reappearance three weeks ago has brought him back to his best

runners.name: Fast Fred
runners.spotlight: Carlisle 1m3f win last July; as good as ever when second at Newcastle (1m4f, AW) one month ago; this is a warm race but he has a competitive mark.
runners.comment: Good second on return one month ago; this is a warm race but he has a competitive mark

runners.name: Haveyoumissedme
runners.spotlight: Absent 348 days before last September but usually competitive since regaining his fitness and went down by a head last month on latest outing; tenth in this race in 2024 on his only appearance at Musselburgh but he's firmly in the mix on other counts.
runners.comment: Tenth in this race in 2024 on his only run here but he's firmly in the mix on other counts

runners.name: Ludo's Landing
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb higher at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last June and returned to form with AW win at Newcastle last month; considered, but the latter win was with first headgear since 2023 and he did not back it up in that campaign.
runners.comment: Returned to form with AW win last month when wearing first headgear since 2023

runners.name: Humble Spark
runners.spotlight: Sometimes starts slowly; 1-11 record on turf is none too persuasive but fourth of 13 in Queen's Cup here (1m6f, soft) last week was creditable; occasionally hooded and now tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 1-11 on turf; creditable fourth in Queen's Cup here (1m6f, soft) last week; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Simple Star
runners.spotlight: Plentiful form at Musselburgh with three C&D wins, including last August for his only Flat win last year (5lb higher today); has a mixed reappearance record but went close in this race in 2024, so has to be considered.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins; has a mixed reappearance record but went close in this race in 2024

runners.name: Sixpack
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose at Goodwood (1m6f, good; 2lb lower today) last May but that's far and away the best of his six runs for James Owen, who won this race in 2024 and 2025; beaten 24l when evens for hurdling debut in October when last seen; needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose last May off 2lb higher, far and away the best of six runs for James Owen

runners.name: Velvet Whisper
runners.spotlight: Back to life in the autumn on last three starts, beaten a neck on first occasion (good to soft) before front-running wins at Nottingham (1m2f, heavy) and Musselburgh (1m4f, soft); the ground may not be so testing for this reappearance but she's relatively lightly raced and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Back to life in the autumn, relatively lightly raced and could have more to offer
runners.owner: C Wright, D Kilburn, A Dick And P Snook

runners.name: Terrorise
runners.spotlight: Won a novice hurdle last May and convincingly at York (2m, good to firm; 4lb higher today) in July; however, comfortably held twice off his revised mark, before a heavy defeat in October when last seen; had wind surgery last month and perhaps best watched this time.
runners.comment: 2m winner last July; had wind surgery last month and perhaps best watched this time

runners.name: Northwest Passage
runners.spotlight: Considerable improvement over 1m2f in the mud once gelded last August, but well beaten in both his hurdle races this winter; this is first run since leaving Roger Teal for 31,000gns in February and given wind surgery; not as solid as some of these but stable is among the winners.
runners.comment: Did well at 1m2f in the mud, then well beaten hurdling; wind surgery since 31,000gns buy

runners.name: Fox Journey
runners.spotlight: No win since May 2024 when trained by Sir Michael Stoute; below form last year for Kevin Ryan, followed by a 22,000gns sale in October; he's 23lb below his peak mark but the tide needs to turn.
runners.comment: Below form last year for Kevin Ryan; 22,000gns buy in October; 23lb below his peak mark

races.race_name: Sky Bet Proud To Support Young Lives Vs Cancer Handicap

runners.name: Sea Suite
runners.spotlight: Had shown ability before making it ninth-time lucky after a 141-day absence at Southwell (7f, AW) last month; only 2lb higher and should have no problem with the return to turf; high on list.
runners.comment: Off the mark at Southwell last month; only 2lb higher and no problem with return to turf

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: Won his first three starts over 7f/1m on the AW for Roger Varian, but placed just once in 13 outings since; 24lb lower than when last on turf, but he still has plenty to prove.
runners.comment: Won his first three starts on the AW for Roger Varian, but placed once in 13 outings since

runners.name: Ziggy's Condor
runners.spotlight: Just 1lb higher than when winning at Newcastle (6f, AW) in January; return to turf not a problem and has run well over 7f including here, but the trip does appear to just stretch him.
runners.comment: Just 1lb above last winning mark; has run well over 7f, but it does appear to stretch him

runners.name: Cargin Bhui
runners.spotlight: In good form on the AW this year including two wins over 6f; 0-12 on turf, but has gone close on a couple of occasions and should have no problem with this sharp 7f; player.
runners.comment: 0-12 on turf, but has gone close on a couple of occasions; no problem with this sharp 7f

runners.name: Benacre
runners.spotlight: Form has an up and down look to it these days and hasn't won on turf since he was a 2yo; back off his last winning mark, but not quite sure what to expect.
runners.comment: Hasn't won on turf since 2022; back off last winning mark, but not sure what to expect

runners.name: They All Know Me
runners.spotlight: 0-7, but was finishing unplaced for the first time when eighth of 11 behind Benacre at Newcastle (7f, AW) in February; cheekpieces removed after one use; needs to bounce back; stable also runs Ziggy's Condor.
runners.comment: 0-7 but unplaced for the first time on latest start; needs to bounce back

runners.name: Invincible Ruby
runners.spotlight: Improved to get off the mark at the sixth attempt at Catterick (7f, good) last July, but regressed in three subsequent outings; returns from seven months off with a bit to prove; stable also runs Qazaq.
runners.comment: Regressed in her last three outings; return from seven months off with a bit to prove

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: 0-8 albeit placed four times, though her very best form has come on the AW; makes her debut for another new yard after six months off having been picked up for 24,000gns in December; may need further than this now and probably best watched.
runners.comment: 0-8; may need further than this now and probably best watched on stable debut

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: 0-7; returning from five months off and having her first start since wind surgery when fourth of 15 at Thirsk (6f, good) on Friday; each-way claims if building on that.
runners.comment: 0-7; better effort when fourth of 15 at Thirsk on Friday; each-way claims

runners.name: Kelpie Grey
runners.spotlight: 0-14 since winning four of his five starts between October 2023 and May 2024, but is 2-5 around here and very well treated on form as recently as last summer; off five months, but is proven fresh.
runners.comment: 2-5 around here and very well treated on form as recently as last summer; proven fresh

runners.name: Abduction
runners.spotlight: 6lb lower than when winning from a poor draw at Chester (7.6f, good to firm) last August and just 1lb higher than when successful at Newcastle (7f, AW) in January; ran well when a close fourth of 12 at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) last time and capable of playing a part; stable also runs Kelpie Grey.
runners.comment: 6lb lower than for his latest success on turf; capable of playing a part

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: Campaigned mostly on AW, winning four times; bled from nose latest start; 0-6 on turf but finished third off 10lb higher in both attempts over C&D; not dismissed.
runners.comment: Four AW wins; finished third off 10lb higher in both C&D attempts; not ruled out

runners.name: Kinetic Force
runners.spotlight: Two wins (AW/turf) for previous yard; close fourth at Newcastle (5f, AW) on stable/seasonal debut; fighting chance provided he builds on that effort; similar type to Emerald Harmony.
runners.comment: Close fourth at Newcastle on stable/seasonal debut; similar type to Emerald Harmony

runners.name: Montezuma
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden whose two attempts over 5f comprise second-place finishes at Musselburgh, the latest eight days ago on seasonal debut when an eye-catcher amid traffic issues; could well go one better off the same mark, provided this pans out better.
runners.comment: Nine-race maiden; eye-catching second over C&D last weekend; could well go one better
runners.owner: B Jordan, Brian Jordan, S Jordan & N McConnell

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Eight wins; close fourth at Doncaster on reappearance but his chance of repeating or bettering that effort depends on the weather's actions; ideally needs ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Chance depends on the weather's actions; ideally needs ground softer than good

runners.name: Arnhem
runners.spotlight: Veteran whose form has dipped in last two outings, latest over C&D eight days ago; doesn't look as if he's about to improve his poor strike-rate (long time between drinks).
runners.comment: Veteran whose form has dipped in last two outings, latest over C&D; poor strike-rate

runners.name: South Parade
runners.spotlight: Went close on reappearance last term but had ended the 2024 campaign in good form, which differs from the way she concluded 2025; not sure what to expect on seasonal debut.
runners.comment: Not sure what to expect on seasonal debut; others are higher in calculations
runners.owner: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd

runners.name: Jm Jhingree
runners.spotlight: Scored three times, including over C&D, during his spell with Jim Goldie; remains in same ownership; returns from 98-day break on debut for new stable; yet to win when fresh.
runners.comment: Returns from 98-day break on debut for new stable; yet to win when fresh

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Placed over C&D last week on reappearance, while leaving the impression he'll come on for the run; ties in closely with Montezuma on that bare form; nicely handicapped, having been successful twice off current mark; one for the shortlist.
runners.comment: Ties in with Montezuma on C&D reappearance form; successful twice off current mark

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Registered his last success in C&D event (off just 2lb lower) in October; quite interesting returned to this scenario with Doncaster reappearance (midfield) under his belt.
runners.comment: Quite interesting returned to this C&D with Doncaster reappearance under his belt

runners.name: Classy Al
runners.spotlight: Posted a duck egg over C&D last weekend on reappearance, taking Musselburgh record to 0-8; has done all his winning at Ayr and Hamilton; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Posted a duck egg over C&D on reappearance; has gained all wins at Ayr/Hamilton

runners.name: Emerald Harmony
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins in Ireland; traded at 1.01 in running, only to be nabbed on the line at Newcastle (5f, AW) on stable/seasonal debut; possibilities, provided she builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Traded at 1.01 en route to going close at Newcastle on stable/seasonal debut; in the mix

runners.name: Pop Star
runners.spotlight: Well treated if proving resurgent but otherwise looks far from solid on his form for current yard, most recently always towards the rear in C&D contest last weekend.
runners.comment: Far from solid on his form for current yard; always towards rear over C&D last time

runners.name: Right And Exact
runners.spotlight: Solid record (122) in 5f novice events in his first season last year, the success at Catterick on good ground; plausibly open to progress in handicaps this season; respected.
runners.comment: Solid record (122) in novice events last term; open to progress in handicaps this season

runners.name: Equity Law
runners.spotlight: Took a while to hit winning form last term for new stable; probably best watched off current mark on 2026 reappearance, unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Pattern shown last term for new stable suggests he's best watched on 2026 reappearance

runners.name: Zarzyni
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, including twice off higher marks in the month of April; unlucky third, having found daylight too late, in this race 12 months ago; soft ground was against him here last weekend; likely player if the cards drop right.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner; unlucky third in this race 12 months ago; enters calculations

runners.name: Northern Spirit
runners.spotlight: Effective on AW and turf; respectable third in big field at Doncaster (6f) returned to this sphere last time; remains on a workable mark and gained last two wins over 5f.
runners.comment: Respectable third in big field at Doncaster on the return to turf; on a workable mark
runners.owner: Steel A Win & Spring Cottage Syndicate 3

runners.name: Blind Beggar
runners.spotlight: Record of 24050 for new yard reflects that he hasn't been able to hold his form; failed to beat a rival in sole outing this year; others have better claims.
runners.comment: Unable to hold his form for new yard; failed to beat a rival when last seen
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win

races.race_name: Visit Bridles Care Home Wincanton Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Special John
runners.spotlight: Hit the ground running for this yard with two chase wins in the autumn (3m/3m2f, good to soft) and then ran pretty well in a marathon chase at Windsor; latest defeat was over hurdles and is probably best forgiven.
runners.comment: Figures of 115 over fences for this yard; below-par run on latest start was over hurdles

runners.name: Mahler Moon
runners.spotlight: Won 3m handicap hurdle at Newbury last March on good to soft; dug deepest to get off the mark over fences at Huntingdon (2m7f, good) and ran well last week at Exeter, for all that only two got round (he was hampered by the faller).
runners.comment: Ran well last week at Exeter, for all that only two got round (hampered by the faller)

runners.name: Masterdream
runners.spotlight: Still on a competitive mark despite winning three times over fences already this season and had a break since a poor performance last time in October; good or good to soft is fine.
runners.comment: Back from a break; competitive mark despite three chase wins this season

runners.name: Byzantium
runners.spotlight: All three wins on good ground; won a 2m3f handicap at Warwick pretty easily and followed up back in novice company; well held since but he was returning from a lengthy break when pulling way too hard to Ludlow last month (2m5f); will need to settle if he's to stay this far but Harry Cobden takes over.
runners.comment: All wins on good ground; pulled hard over shorter last time but now Cobden takes over

runners.name: Sinchi Roca
runners.spotlight: Point winner on good ground over 2m4f; bumper winner on heavy and won his maiden here on soft (1m7f); nearly went in again in a Taunton novice last time but this is a huge hike in trip for only his second handicap, having never raced beyond 2m1f under rules.
runners.comment: Form respected but he's never raced beyond 2m1f under rules

runners.name: Kitty Foyle
runners.spotlight: Flat/hurdle winner; headed to Kempton last time unproven over this far but saw it out fine to finish third of the nine runners (on good ground); solid chance off 1lb higher.
runners.comment: Headed to Kempton last time unproven over this far but saw it out fine to finish third

races.race_name: Bridles Care Home In Wincanton Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Siorai
runners.spotlight: Some promise in 1m2f/1m4f maidens for Jim Bolger; has raced freely in both her races over hurdles, despite the hood going on last time at Stratford where she was easily the best of the rest behind a long odds-on winner; a 4yo filly receiving all the allowances and looks hard to beat.
runners.comment: Easily best of the rest at Stratford when second to a long odds-on winner; the one to beat

runners.name: Eternity Rose
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in three bumpers but has yet to get anything going over hurdles; could do better in handicaps and he'll get a mark after this, but probably still has a place chance.
runners.comment: Made frame in three bumpers; place chance despite yet to get anything going over hurdles

runners.name: La Cadalora
runners.spotlight: Showed ability without winning for James Fanshawe and stayed 1m6f; never counted on her hurdling debut here (good to soft; 28-1) and a tongue-tie now joins the regular hood; could fare better.
runners.comment: Respected on Flat form as she'll stay okay; tongue-tie fitted after quiet hurdle debut

runners.name: Pivotal Days
runners.spotlight: 7f winner on heavy ground for Ed Walker, his only win in 11 runs for that yard; joined this one for 7,500gns and finished last in a 1m Class 5 handicap at Chelmsford a month ago; not bred for hurdles and might not stay.
runners.comment: 1-12 on the Flat; not bred for hurdles and might not stay

runners.name: Blue Siam
runners.spotlight: Modest Flat maiden; well behind Siorai when tailed off at Stratford two weeks ago; hood added.
runners.comment: Modest Flat maiden; behind Siorai when tailed off at Stratford two weeks ago; hood added

runners.name: Trooper
runners.spotlight: 5-25 during Flat career on the continent so has ability, but hasn't shown any of it in two runs over hurdles.
runners.comment: Plenty of ability on the Flat but hasn't shown any of it in two runs over hurdles

runners.name: Louis Des Landes
runners.spotlight: Pretty much tailed off in a bumper (good to firm) and on hurdle debut at Taunton (heavy); hard to make a case.
runners.comment: Pretty much tailed off in a bumper (good to firm) and on hurdle debut at Taunton (heavy)

races.race_name: Bridles New Luxury Care Home Handicap Chase

runners.name: Calgary Tiger
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who hasn't offered a great deal since returning from nearly a year out and it's seldom easy for veterans to rediscover their form; however, this is a much easier race than he's accustomed to.
runners.comment: Offered little following a year out but this is a much easier race than he's accustomed to

runners.name: My Rockstar
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-16 over fences; beaten about 11l in two 2m4f handicaps here since an absence and there are positives to take out of both those runs; this drop back in trip could help.
runners.comment: A weakener in 2m4f chases here on last two outings; the drop back in trip could help

runners.name: No Guarantee
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner and only 2lb higher than for his win at Hereford in December; however, didn't look a natural on last month's chase debut and was pulled up.
runners.comment: Capable hurdler but didn't look a natural on last month's chase debut and was pulled up

runners.name: Redbridge Rambler
runners.spotlight: Suited by good or quicker ground; similar sort of form in his last four races in handicaps from 2m4f to 3m1f; this trip is on the tight side but he's in a weak race and might be ridden forcefully.
runners.comment: This trip is on the tight side but he's in a weak race and might be ridden forcefully

runners.name: Richforadaay
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden but he was backed on last month's chase/handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good) and ran well to a point (beaten 14l); could build on that.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l on chase/handicap debut but was backed and ran well to a point

runners.name: Daany
runners.spotlight: Three-time Flat winner and successful four times over hurdles; however, hasn't been running all that well under either code following a year on the sidelines and this is a belated first attempt at chasing.
runners.comment: Struggled under both codes after a year out; this is a belated first attempt at chasing
runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

races.race_name: Bridles Care Home In Wincanton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Time To Bite
runners.spotlight: Most wins and best form are over fences and he ran pretty well at Exeter last month; the time before, in October, he won a small-field handicap hurdle at Exeter off his much lower hurdle mark (now 3lb higher); could have some part to play.
runners.comment: Most wins and best form are over fences but he did win his last race over hurdles

runners.name: Nardaran
runners.spotlight: Won two small-field handicaps at 2m (good to soft) in the autumn; hasn't kicked on, though latest run at Kempton was more encouraging and 2m5f is worth persevering with.
runners.comment: Latest run at Kempton was more encouraging and 2m5f is worth persevering with

runners.name: Reallyntruthfully
runners.spotlight: Chase campaign aborted after two forgettable efforts since a winning start in October (2m3f); on a fair mark for this return to hurdles and has a strike-rate of 2-6 in this discipline.
runners.comment: On a fair mark for this return to hurdles and has a strike-rate of 2-6 in this discipline

runners.name: John Barbour
runners.spotlight: Has done well since raised to 2m4f and narrowly beaten at Ascot on his penultimate start by one who was well handicapped; beaten too far out at Uttoxeter last time to blame the longer trip (2m7f) but definite chance at this level if he's back on his game.
runners.comment: First blip for a while last time at Uttoxeter; definite chance if back on song

runners.name: Doc McCoy
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 5-12 over hurdles and won easily at Warwick (2m5f, good) before pulling up at Ascot in October; he does have a bit to prove off this mark even if returning in good order.
runners.comment: Off since October; bit to prove off this mark even if returning in good order

runners.name: Khafre
runners.spotlight: Seems to be improving having followed up his novice success with another 2m3f win at Taunton 19 days ago (handicap debut; good); Paddy Hanlon rides again off a 5lb higher mark; this 3m Irish point winner is much respected.
runners.comment: Back-to-back wins at Taunton, the latest on handicap debut; looking progressive

runners.name: Obsessedwithyou
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-6 over fences when winning easily in a small field at Sedgefield in October (2m) and later that month she bumped into the useful Bluey (whom Queens Gamble beat for her last win) at Kempton (2m2f, good); chance at these weights if fitness isn't an issue.
runners.comment: 3-7 over fences and has a chance at these weights if fitness isn't an issue
runners.owner: Awr Consultancy Ltd & The Folly Partners

runners.name: Somespring Special
runners.spotlight: 4-14 over fences; October's Chepstow win (2m3f, good) triggered this career-high mark, off which she was found wanting by some way at Kempton, admittedly in a useful handicap; this shorter trip is another slight query.
runners.comment: Likeable mare but on a career-high mark and this trip is another slight query

runners.name: Queens Gamble
runners.spotlight: A winner at Listed level in bumpers, over hurdles and fences; jumped particularly well when flooring odds-on Bluey here in February (heavy) and again ran well behind a sharp improver at Doncaster six weeks ago (soft; off 1lb higher); has won on good ground; very solid.
runners.comment: A winner at Listed level in bumpers, over hurdles and fences; recent efforts very solid

runners.name: Northern Air
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle for Fergal O'Brien in early 2025; three disappointing chase runs for Dan Skelton leave her with questions to answer in this discipline and, although she steadied the ship with a second back over hurdles at Newton Abbot, that was only a Class 5 handicap.
runners.comment: Opposable on chase form; recent 2nd over hurdles was in a Class 5 handicap

races.race_name: Visit Bridles Care Home Wincanton Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Ugo Bingo
runners.spotlight: Has slipped to a good mark but didn't really find any improvement (based on early-season form) for this new headgear combination when a wilting fourth here last month; needs to find extra.
runners.comment: A wilting fourth in this new headgear here last time and remains below his best

runners.name: Postponed Legacy
runners.spotlight: Very progressive until a laboured performance at Taunton (good) five weeks ago when he raced freely and jumped poorly; he's better than that but could now be on a challenging mark.
runners.comment: Better than he showed last time but could now be on a challenging mark

runners.name: Prince Rhinegold
runners.spotlight: Had Ugo Bingo behind when second over C&D last month but his run here 11 days ago was pretty miserable and he can be up and down.
runners.comment: Second here last month but a pretty miserable run 11 days ago; can be up and down
runners.owner: Mrs D Robinson Mrs & Mrs Livesey-Van Dorst

runners.name: Saucats
