runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden over hurdles; it wasn't a bad run behind Victory Shout at Taunton but there's no obvious reason why he should reverse those placings.
runners.comment: Behind Victory Shout at Taunton and no obvious reason why he should reverse those placings

runners.name: Little Lady Lucy
runners.spotlight: Flat winner; 0-11 over hurdles and achieved zilch in three runs after an absence.
runners.comment: Flat winner; 0-11 over hurdles and achieved zilch in three runs after an absence

runners.name: Ribba Hill
runners.spotlight: Flat winner who is 0-6 over hurdles but had his favoured good ground when second over 2m4f here three weeks ago and the well-backed winner followed up in easy fashion at Ludlow; drops in trip but has a big shout off 1lb higher provided the rain stays away.
runners.comment: Needs good ground; second here last time and the winner has bolted up since
runners.owner: Syd Hosie Racing Syndicate - Ribba Hill

runners.name: Anthracite
runners.spotlight: Little worthwhile form under Flat or hurdle codes; 80-1 when well behind Victory Shout last time at Taunton; now tries cheekpieces.
runners.comment: 80-1 when well behind Victory Shout last time at Taunton; now tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Victory Shout
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful on the Flat at his best, superior to stablemate Ribba Hill; ridden out to win at Taunton (2m, good) after a break and the winner got away from him (he was beaten 12l) when he was penalised here over 2m4f; can be given another chance despite another 2lb, especially back in trip.
runners.comment: Won at Taunton; the winner got away from him when second here 11 days ago

runners.name: Forest Hills
runners.spotlight: 1m2f Flat winner who has been completely tailed off in all three runs over hurdles; at least goes handicapping off a lowly mark and drying ground would help matters, so watch the weather.
runners.comment: 1m2f Flat winner who has been tailed off in three runs over hurdles; handicap debut

races.race_name: Castle Cary Mares' Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Western Charm
runners.spotlight: Quite free when drawing clear to win on heavy ground here in January but that was even more the case when tailed off in stronger company at Sandown; needs to settle down but has major form claims at this level.
runners.comment: Free-going tendencies remain a worry but she has strong form claims at this level

runners.name: Sorgean
runners.spotlight: Bought for £38,000 after finishing second in a 3m Irish point on yielding ground; the winner is unraced for Emmet Mullins.
runners.comment: Bought for £38,000 after finishing second in a 3m Irish point on yielding ground

runners.name: Hollie Potter
runners.spotlight: The well-related dam won a Listed bumper and 2m3f hurdle; she's kicking off in a modest race and stable has occasional success in bumpers.
runners.comment: The well-related dam won a Listed bumper and 2m3f hurdle; kicks off in a modest race

runners.name: Princess Of Ballea
runners.spotlight: Third at Huntingdon and fifth at Stratford, beaten about 6l and 11l respectively; needs better, but has raced freely (hooded both times) and Harry Cobden takes over from a claimer.
runners.comment: Has raced freely in both races, so didn't perform badly; Cobden replaces a claimer

runners.name: Lady Litigator
runners.spotlight: Newbury debut winner who was in contention when unseating next time; disappointed back at Newbury in late February and jockey bookings suggest that Western Charm is the yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Debut winner; well held last time and looks the yard second string on jockey bookings

runners.name: Miss Doyenne
runners.spotlight: Point (beating Cool Native) and bumper winner was third in a C&D maiden hurdle in December but was a poor third at Punchestown (hvy) since; this better ground should help her bounce back and is a big player if doing so even with an 8lb penalty.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D maiden hurdle but a poor third at Punchestown since; better ground can help

runners.name: Cool Native
runners.spotlight: Point winner last year was previously runner-up to Miss Doyenne in that sphere (beaten 1.75l); solid third in a Navan maiden hurdle on rules debut; unseated halfway last week; there shouldn't be much between her and the former.
runners.comment: Third in a Navan maiden hurdle; unseated last week; closely matched with Miss Doyenne

runners.name: Whispering Hopes
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose maiden had been placed over hurdles for Gordon Elliott but has been poor in two starts for this yard (both here) and needs improvement on her best form anyway.
runners.comment: Poor in two starts for this yard (both here) and needs improvement on her best form anyway

runners.name: Forever And A Day
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles and a bumper so best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maiden hurdles and a bumper so best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Mercurial Sea
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper but has been well-held in four hurdles including on handicap debut last time; rated 90 so needs lots more.
runners.comment: Well-held in four hurdles including on h'cap debut last time; rated 90 so needs lots more

runners.name: Infinity Succes
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in an Academy hurdle on debut but has been below that level in two bumpers since; has to resume progress.
runners.comment: Decent run in an Academy hurdle on debut but below that level in two bumpers since

runners.name: Hathaways Cottage
runners.spotlight: Five-time point winner was fourth in a hunter chase here last month but was pulled-up in a point since; hood added; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Fourth in a hunter chase here last month but pulled-up in a point since; others preferred

runners.name: Likeyouhaveplenty
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 2m hurdle winner, out of dual bumper winner; not a bad pedigree and could run respectably on debut.
runners.comment: Not a bad pedigree and could run respectably on debut

runners.name: Creevybeg
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to winners Tackletommywoowoo (3m hurdle) and Redwood Queen (2m-2m4f hurdle); dam unraced sister to 2m hurdle winner Play The Market; Eur1,200 foal is a sibling to a pair of smart sorts so could run respectably on debut.
runners.comment: Is a sibling to a pair of smart sorts so could run respectably on debut

runners.name: The Poet's Way
runners.spotlight: Hasn't jumped well when pulled-up in two maiden hurdles so passed over today.
runners.comment: Hasn't jumped well when pulled-up in two maiden hurdles so passed over today

runners.name: Ri Ra
runners.spotlight: Late starter was beaten 24l in both bumper runs and is best watched for now.
runners.comment: Late starter was beaten 24l in both bumper runs and is best watched for now

runners.name: Lady Cosmo
runners.spotlight: Point winner in November but was beaten a long way in a Punchestown maiden hurdle since and best watched for now.
runners.comment: Point winner was beaten a long way in a Punchestown maiden hurdle and best watched for now
runners.owner: Mrs Rose Boyd, Mrs Marie J. Armstrong, Seamus Carmichael

runners.name: Thelittlevillager
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in two maiden hurdles so look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in two maiden hurdles so look elsewhere

runners.name: Westin Pimpernel
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off and pulled-up in maiden hurdles so passed over today.
runners.comment: Tailed-off and pulled-up in maiden hurdles so passed over today

runners.name: Shortt Fuse
runners.spotlight: Not a lot to show for three maiden hurdle efforts at huge prices and passed over today.
runners.comment: Not a lot to show for three maiden hurdle efforts at huge prices and passed over today

runners.name: Eitilt
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles and best look elsewhere today.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three maiden hurdles and best look elsewhere today

runners.name: Supreme Axel
runners.spotlight: Not much promise in two points and two maiden hurdles so passed over today.
runners.comment: Not much promise in two points and two maiden hurdles so passed over today

runners.name: Pleasureofpressure
runners.spotlight: Maiden has been consistent in handicaps over the last year; not his best run when a beaten favourite over a bit further here last time but can go closer if improving from that.
runners.comment: Not his best run when a beaten favourite over a bit further here last time; can go closer

runners.name: Party Pack
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; maiden produced his best run when second in a 2m1f Wexford handicap last August; not a bad effort on comeback at Cork earlier this week; could run well upped in trip.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort on comeback at Cork earlier this week; could run well

runners.name: Brother Hollie
runners.spotlight: The best of three maiden hurdle runs came at Navan on second start; tongue-tie is back on along with the hood worn last time; needs to improve.
runners.comment: The best of three maiden hurdle runs came at Navan on second start; needs to improve

runners.name: Kalsman
runners.spotlight: Sole hurdle win came at Downpatrick in 2024 over 2m6f; only two runs since then, both decent efforts over that same C&D, the latest two weeks ago; a player with improvement from that.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort on comeback two weeks ago; a player with improvement from that

runners.name: Dairy Force
runners.spotlight: Won a Kilbeggan 2m maiden hurdle in 2024 for Liam O'Brien; no win since but plenty of solid efforts including the last twice for this yard; up 3lb but should go close with a repeat.
runners.comment: Decent runs the last twice for this yard; up 3lb but should go close with a repeat

runners.name: Circus Act
runners.spotlight: Won her second hurdle race when winning this race a year ago off 2lb lower; has only run four times between the Flat and hurdles since; should run well with a repeat of best; ground-versatile.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 2lb lower; should run well with a repeat of best; ground-versatile

runners.name: Queen Nel
runners.spotlight: Won a Gowran maiden hurdle over 2m in 2024; no win since and isn't easy to fancy on her poor recent form.
runners.comment: No win since 2024 and isn't easy to fancy on her poor recent form

runners.name: Justicialism
runners.spotlight: Hasn't won over hurdles for just over four years; has been poor on last three starts and needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Hasn't won over hurdles for just over four years; has been poor on last three starts

runners.name: Boston Fire
runners.spotlight: Hasn't shown much ability in points and maiden hurdles so not easy to fancy even dropping into handicaps; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown much ability in points and maiden hurdles so not easy to fancy

runners.name: Urbs Intacta Manet
runners.spotlight: Not a lot of promise in four maiden hurdles, including for this yard last time; has to do a lot more even dropping into handicaps.
runners.comment: Not a lot of promise in four maidens; has to do a lot more even dropping into handicaps

runners.name: Kp's Ladyofknock
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m Clonmel handicap in 2024 and a solid third over 2m1f at this track when last seen; has an absence to overcome.
runners.comment: Solid third over 2m1f at this track when last seen; has an absence to overcome

races.race_name: Fast As Fibrus Broadband Novice Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: King Gris
runners.spotlight: Improved form since switched to this yard, winning handicaps at Ayr and Hexham over approx 2m4f in testing ground; this is more competitive and on different ground but has to be respected.
runners.comment: Won consecutive handicaps in UK; this is more competitive but he has to be respected

runners.name: Hitthehayson
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner won consecutive handicap chases over the winter at Fairyhouse and Haydock; not as good in two hurdle runs since and has to return to best.
runners.comment: Won two chases over the winter; not as good in two hurdle runs since; has to rebound

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Won consecutive handicap chases at Clonmel in October; was badly hampered when beaten 14l over hurdles at the same track on recent return; can do better now so one to consider.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l over hurdles at Clonmel on recent return; can do better now so one to consider
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Irish Champ
runners.spotlight: Point winner has run creditably in several maiden hurdles; not a bad effort on handicap debut over 2m4f here last time but has to do more off the same mark.
runners.comment: Not a bad effort on handicap debut over 2m4f here last time but has to do more

runners.name: Only For Our Man
runners.spotlight: Point winner was second in a bumper last year; best effort over hurdles came when second in a 2m3f Naas handicap last time; handles good ground and looks a major player if staying this trip.
runners.comment: Second in a 2m3f Naas handicap last time and looks a major player if staying this trip

runners.name: Stardhem
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner in UK for Ben Pauling but lost his form after; bounced back on first start for this yard when scoring over hurdles at Cork (3m); raised 4lb but the hood worn then is retained; respected
runners.comment: Won over hurdles at Cork (3m); raised 4lb but the hood worn then is retained; respected

runners.name: Couldntmakeitup
runners.spotlight: Maiden was only beaten 4l in third in a 2m Punchestown handicap in January; stumbled halfway when was poor at Newbury since; is a player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Stumbled halfway when was poor at Newbury last time; is a player if bouncing back

runners.name: Dark Lieutenant
runners.spotlight: Some promise in bumpers; was badly hampered when well beaten on handicap hurdle debut last time at Clonmel; can do better and sounder surface can help.
runners.comment: Badly hampered when well beaten on handicap hurdle debut last time; can do better

runners.name: Jukebox Johnny
runners.spotlight: Best run when a 10l fourth in a Cork handicap over 2m4f in testing ground in December; below that level next time and pulled-up in cheekpieces since; questions now.
runners.comment: Best run at Cork last December; pulled-up in cheekpieces last time; questions now
runners.owner: Anthony N Durkan & B M Durkan

runners.name: Mistyburn
runners.spotlight: Point winner hasn't progressed under rules but ran a decent race on handicap bow when third over 3m at Cork last time; this shorter trip should suit better; a player.
runners.comment: Third over 3m at Cork last time; this shorter trip should suit better; a player

runners.name: Rough 'n' Smooth
runners.spotlight: The best of three maiden hurdle runs came at Fairyhouse last time over 2m4f; up in trip on handicap debut and can run well with more likely to come.
runners.comment: Steps up in trip on handicap debut and can run well with more likely to come

runners.name: Danny The Champ
runners.spotlight: Maiden hasn't shown any progress since switched to handicaps and has to find improvement from somewhere.
runners.comment: No progress since switched to handicaps and has to find improvement from somewhere

races.race_name: Fibrus Says Neigh To Price Hikes Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Wakey Wakey Man
runners.spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles but placed in two big-field handicaps for new stable since December, more recently when clear second behind well-backed rival at Fairyhouse in January (2m4f, soft); a bit below his best when fourth at Ludlow last time (2m5f, good to soft); capable of getting involved.
runners.comment: Placed in two big-field h'caps since joining this stable; capable of getting involved

runners.name: Coppola
runners.spotlight: Maiden point-to-pointer has improved in two runs for Gordon Elliott; finished runner-up here last month (2m5f, yielding) when beaten just 0.75l and keeping on; filled the same position at Downpatrick two weeks ago (2m6f, yielding to soft) when 7.5l behind the reopposing Cozone; 9lb swing with that rival now; big player.
runners.comment: Improved in two runs for Elliott; 7.5l behind Cozone in second last time but 9lb swing now

runners.name: Knocknagoran Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed mild promise last season prior to being pulled up on her final start here; no telling impression when a well-beaten seventh on seasonal return at Fairyhouse in January (2m4f, soft); was a somewhat surprising 100-30 favourite at Naas last time when tailed off; new headgear is reached for now.
runners.comment: No impression on seasonal return and tailed off when surprisingly sent off fav' last time

runners.name: Cozone
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form with a bang at Downpatrick (2m6f, yielding to soft) when defeating runner-up Coppola by 7.5l off a career low mark; hefty 10lb hit for that, but this three-time hurdles winner has won off a mark of 106 before; big player.
runners.comment: Back to form with a bang last time; 10lb hike but has won off higher; big player

runners.name: Duce Bigalow
runners.spotlight: Consistent over fences recently; finished second at Naas in December (2m, heavy) and bounced back from a below par run with two runner-up finishes in his last two starts at Navan (3m, soft to heavy) and Fairyhouse (3m, heavy); makes stable debut back over hurdles off a 9lb lower mark, but may not be as effective over timber and ground also a question.
runners.comment: Consistent over fences; runs from 9lb lower hurdles mark but better ground a worry
runners.owner: James Woods & A M Ryan & Donie Culloty & D Enright

runners.name: Imbolc
runners.spotlight: Is 0-17 (0-13 over hurdles) and recent form doesn't suggest he's ready to shed his maiden tag.
runners.comment: No wins from 13 over hurdles and recent form not encouraging

runners.name: Head For Home
runners.spotlight: Took a big step forward off reduced mark in first-time cheekpieces at Punchestown in October when second to a clear winner (2m4f, good to yielding); respectable enough effort on soft ground at Tramore last time when fifth (2m5f); trainer in better form now, so not discounted.
runners.comment: Big step forward when second in Oct'; okay fifth next time; trainer in better form now

runners.name: John Storey
runners.spotlight: Big-field Fairyhouse handicap hurdle winner on New Year's Day 2025 off 1lb higher (3m1f, yielding to soft); well beaten in final starts for Liam Cusack, but his fourth at Punchestown on stable debut was a positive (2m7f, heavy); 100-30 favourite at Downpatrick last time but was never travelling; questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Positive stable debut when fourth but never travelling when fav' latest; questions now

runners.name: Take Chance
runners.spotlight: Decent runs in defeat over autumn/winter, finishing fourth here (3m1f, good to yielding) and third at Punchestown (3m, heavy); less than fluent jumping at Musselburgh when fifth in February (3m, good to soft); stayed on for a distant third at Clonmel last time (3m, heavy); not out of it and this better ground may help.
runners.comment: Some decent runs in defeat this season; not out of it and the ground may help

runners.name: Georginas Jet
runners.spotlight: Placed in three successive races in August at 2m2f-22.5f; unseen since being pulled up at Listowel in September (2m6f, good to yielding) when wearing a first-time tongue tie, which is now left off; others preferred.
runners.comment: Placed three times in Aug'; unseen since PU at Listowel in Sept; others preferred

runners.name: Kellys Corner
runners.spotlight: Best effort when third in a 16-runner Naas maiden hurdle in December (2m3f, soft to heavy); solid fifth in beginners' chase debut at Cork; pulled up at Punchestown next time, but bounced back with a solid staying on fourth at Gowran last time (2m4f, heavy) in this headgear; has done most of his racing on softer.
runners.comment: Fourth at Gowran last time in this headgear; has done most of his racing on softer

runners.name: Lecale's Diamond
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on handicap hurdle debut at Navan off 1lb higher (2m, heavy); reverts to hurdles now after some creditable performances in two beginners' chases; may do better now trying this trip over hurdles.
runners.comment: Tailed off on h'cap debut but may do better after creditable runs over fences recently

runners.name: Ardera Gun
runners.spotlight: Has failed to finish closer than tenth in three handicap hurdle attempts; up against it; new headgear tried.
runners.comment: Has failed to finish closer than tenth in three handicap hurdle attempts; up against it

runners.name: Joya Del Mar
runners.spotlight: No wins from 21 starts but some good efforts all the same, including when a close second here in a 2023 maiden (2m4f, soft); returned from an absence here last time when keeping on for a 14.5l fourth (2m5f, yielding); that should have brought her on.
runners.comment: 0-21 but some good efforts all the same; kept on for 4th here after an absence last time
runners.owner: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan

runners.name: Diaduit Buachaill
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden form and similarly moderate on handicap debut here last month (2m5f, yielding); others more appealing.
runners.comment: Modest m'den form and similarly moderate on h'cap debut here; others more appealing

runners.name: Peppers Corner
runners.spotlight: First reserve; point-to-point winner didn't set the world alight in maiden hurdles and no improvement sent handicapping at Limerick last time.
runners.comment: Reserve; didn't set the world alight in m'den hurdles and no improvement on h'cap debut

runners.name: Artic Chief
runners.spotlight: Nothing positive to take from his four maiden runs and he was pulled up at Naas for his first handicap; hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Nothing positive to take from m'dens runs nor h'cap debut last time; hard to fancy

runners.name: Sphagnum
runners.spotlight: Much better chaser, winning 3m4f Cork National last season (good); holds a whopping 30lb lower hurdles mark but unable to capitalise on it in three runs over timber this season; must be kept in mind considering his mark.
runners.comment: Holds a whopping 30lb lower hurdle mark; unable to take advantage in three runs this term

runners.name: Youknowme
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in four maiden hurdles and not much better on handicap debut at Navan when last seen in September; unlikely.
runners.comment: Tailed off in four m'dens and not much better in h'cap when last seen; unlikely

runners.name: Jack Inthe Jukebox
runners.spotlight: Modest form in point-to-points and similar story in three maiden hurdles; makes handicap debut now, but not much to recommend him.
runners.comment: Modest point form and similar story in m'dens; not much to recommend him on h'cap debut

runners.name: Lady Iseult
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over 2m5f at Roscommon over three years ago; absent over two years and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Can only be watched returning from an absence of more than two years

races.race_name: Giddy Up And Switch To Fibrus Broadband Beginners Chase

runners.name: Sandor Clegane
runners.spotlight: Promising novice chaser in 2023-2024 season and ran well in Graded hurdles in winter of 2024-2025; fairly moderate form this season but he shaped better than the bare result in the Kim Muir last time when making smooth headway turning in before his effort petered out (3m3f, good); tongue tie goes back on now and this is a good opportunity.
runners.comment: Has been disappointing but shaped better than the result at Cheltenham; good opportunity
runners.owner: Mrs K Browne & Mrs Anne Coffey

runners.name: Ballybow
runners.spotlight: Useful 128-rated hurdler last season, including a Grade 3 win; well beaten over various trips in four beginners' chases this season, fourth behind King Alexander at Gowran was his best effort; tailed off in the Leinster National at Naas last time when 3m1f on soft to heavy may have been too far; 13lb to find on ratings with Sandor Clegane, but that rival is not the most solid.
runners.comment: Useful 128-rated hurdler has been poor over fences this term; 13lb to find on ratings

runners.name: Written In My Soul
runners.spotlight: Placed in sole point-to-point; finished sixth of 18 on handicap hurdle debut here last month off a mark of 87 (2m5f, yielding); a fair bit to do based on that on ratings for chase debut.
runners.comment: Placed in sole point; sixth in h'cap hurdle debut here off 87 latest; a bit to do

runners.name: Astley
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in only point-to-point; best of six runs under rules was a distant runner-up finish at Clonmel in a bumper (2m4f, good to yielding); has beaten very few rivals in three runs over hurdles and tries fences now.
runners.comment: Tailed off in only point; beaten very few rivals over hurdles and tries fences now

runners.name: Villagers Cross

runners.comment: PU on h'cap hurdle debut and well beaten in claimer latest; unlikely

runners.name: Meet And Greet
runners.spotlight: No wins since December 2024 and three chase efforts to date have not been super encouraging; beaten 23l in a Thurles handicap last time (2m5f, yielding); nonetheless, he's the second highest rated of the field here and possibilities if putting his best foot forward.
runners.comment: Three chase runs have not been encouraging, but rating gives him a good chance if on song

races.race_name: Switch To Fibrus Today And Save Handicap Chase

runners.name: Neon Diamond
runners.spotlight: Bumper and hurdle winner has run well in two of three chase starts; up in trip should suit; not so at Clonmel last time but this better ground will suit so can be a player.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner has run well in two of three chase starts; up in trip should suit; player

runners.name: Brucejack
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner is yet to win over fences in 18 starts but ran a fine race at Clonmel last time over nearly 3m; stepping up in trip will be a help and can be involved off the same mark.
runners.comment: Yet to win over fences in 18 starts but ran a fine race at Clonmel last time; trip a help

runners.name: Beggars Rock
runners.spotlight: Beginners' chase winner last year for Declan Queally but hasn't been as good since for different yards; has to return to best on second start for this stable; better ground a help.
runners.comment: Beginners' chase winner last year but hasn't been as good since and has to return to best

runners.name: Aclass
runners.spotlight: Not at best over hurdles last time at Tramore but won at that venue previously and has been consistent over fences since; one of two runners for the yard and be involved.
runners.comment: Not at best over hurdles last time but previously consistent over fences; be involved

runners.name: Hello Jack
runners.spotlight: Maiden ran well over hurdles at this track last year; has run well enough in h'cap chases at Fairyhouse and Leopardstown latest; needs to find more up in trip.
runners.comment: Maiden has run well enough in two h'cap chases latest; needs to find more up in trip

runners.name: Tonaghneave Well
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins and rated 129 in that sphere; just hints of ability over fences but is off a 10lb lower mark so considered off a break with his yard in better form now.
runners.comment: Two hurdle wins; just hints of ability over fences but off a 10lb lower mark so considered

runners.name: Attaboyarchie
runners.spotlight: Maiden has run well on two of last three outings; however, is a 26-race maiden and has to find more from somewhere to end that sequence; down 2lb.
runners.comment: Maiden has run well on two of last three; has to find more from somewhere

runners.name: The Gradual Slope
runners.spotlight: Course winner over hurdles came back to form when winning the Ladies National at Fairyhouse last week over 2m5f; up 5lb but go well over a trip he stays.
runners.comment: Course winner came back to form when winning at Fairyhouse last week; up 5lb but go well

runners.name: Millie B
runners.spotlight: Four point wins; maiden after six hurdle starts; posted a decent h'cap hurdle effort last time but is 13lb out of the handicap on chasing debut.
runners.comment: Four point wins; decent h'cap hurdle effort last time but a long way out of the handicap

runners.name: Goody Four Shoes
runners.spotlight: Some decent hurdle runs without winning; not bad efforts both chase starts but will have to find improvement for handicap debut.
runners.comment: Some decent hurdle runs without winning; not bad efforts both chase starts; h'cap debut

runners.name: Reve Inoui
runners.spotlight: Point winner returned from a long absence with two poor hurdle runs; however, was much better on chasing debut at Clonmel and could get closer off the same mark if staying this trip.
runners.comment: Not a bad chasing debut at Clonmel and could get closer off same mark if staying trip

runners.name: Gatsby Grey
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner ran well enough over fences at Limerick over Christmas; burst a blood vessel when pulled-up on h'cap chase debut last time and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Burst a blood vessel when pulled-up on h'cap chase debut last time and has to rebound

races.race_name: Fibrus Total Home Wi-Fi Handicap Chase

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Just 1-16 in point-to-points; pulled up in a Thurles hunters' chase in January, but took third at this track next time (2m5f, good to yielding); tailed off in first-time cheekpieces (retained) at Cork last time (3m1f, good to yielding); others preferred.
runners.comment: 1-16 in points; third here in March but PU at Cork latest; others preferred

runners.name: Hardy Diamond
runners.spotlight: A win apiece over hurdles and fences, both at Kilbeggan on good ground; soundly beaten since his last win in August; this better ground will suit her but others make more appeal.
runners.comment: Hurdles/chase winner but soundly beaten since last win in Aug'; others more appealing

runners.name: Snow Punt
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner is 0-15 under rules', but he has been running well in defeat recently; beaten just a nose at Limerick last month (2m7f, heavy); followed that with a runner-up finish at Clonmel last time (2m7f, soft); cheekpieces are added to the tongue tie now, but better ground is a bit concerning.
runners.comment: Point winner is 0-15 under rules' but good runs in defeat last twice; ground concerning

runners.name: Warmer Days Ahead
runners.spotlight: Winner of a 2m5f Roscommon handicap hurdle in 2024 off 92; okay efforts in his opening two runs this season but recent runs are below that level; this is just his second chase start, with his only other seeing him tailed off at Clonmel in a 2024 handicap.
runners.comment: Okay in opening runs this season but below that last twice; just second chase start here

runners.name: Plain Or Battered
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is in good form in both codes recently; 3lb outside the weights over hurdles at Leopardstown, but took the third spot (3m, soft); backed that up when runner-up in a Clonmel novice handicap chase last month (3m, soft); rated 6lb lower in this sphere and should go well again.
runners.comment: In good form recently, placed over hurdles and fences last time; should go well again
runners.owner: David Norris & Fergal Norris & N Dooly

runners.name: La Tene
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; returned from a lengthy absence with three poor runs, but there was definite promise when third at Clonmel (2m3f, soft) last time under a positive ride; tries a new trip for chasing debut now.
runners.comment: Reserve; poor in three runs back from long absence, but definite promise when third latest
runners.owner: John Patrick Ryan & Miss Gillian Ryan

runners.name: Tell Nobody Nothin
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler is 0-9 over fences but has run well in defeat; took third here on St Stephen's Day (2m5f, good to yielding) and 11l behind in fourth last time  (2m5f, yielding to soft); tries this trip for the first time; place chance.
runners.comment: 0-9 over fences but some good runs in defeat inc' here; place chance over new trip

runners.name: Just For Yuse
runners.spotlight: Sole win came on the Flat in Britain; best of nine chase starts was a very remote second at Punchestown in January (2m4f, soft); pulled up and tailed off in two runs since.
runners.comment: Best of nine chase starts was a very remote second at Punchestown in Jan'; unlikely

runners.name: Dinoland
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner, with his most recent win coming here in 2024 (3m, good); pulled up on final start for John McConnell at Tipperary (2m7f, yielding) in July when challenging for the lead before a bad mistake at the final fence; interesting contender if fit enough.
runners.comment: Challenging for lead when mistake at last fence in July; interesting contender if ready
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & J Mackle & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith

runners.name: Royal Time
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner at Naas last season; good effort on his fourth chase start at this track in January (2m5f, yielding to soft) when third of 11; tailed off at Thurles last time; tries this trip for the first time and the jury is out on whether this is what he needs.
runners.comment: Good third here in Jan'; tries this trip for first time but not certain it's what he needs

runners.name: Le Grand Vert
runners.spotlight: Has a liking for Ayr with two of his three chase wins coming there, including in February (3m, soft); that win came after a moderate season; lacked the pace at an important stage at Sedgefield last time (3m3f, good); needs to do it away from Ayr.
runners.comment: Two of three chase wins came at Ayr inc' in Feb'; moderate season prior to that

runners.name: Hit The Frame
runners.spotlight: Landed a Wexford handicap chase (3m1f, good) off 7lb lower in August; still leading when fell at the eleventh at Punchestown when last seen in October (2m7f, good to yielding); absence to overcome but player if that doesn't pose problems.
runners.comment: Wexford h'cap winner in Aug'; absence to overcome but player if ready

runners.name: Tullabrack Lad
runners.spotlight: 0-13 and yet to trouble the judge; not much encouragement in three handicap chase runs at huge odds since returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: 0-13 and yet to trouble the judge; not much encouragement in three recent h'cap chase runs

runners.name: Cordal Boy
runners.spotlight: Good effort on stable debut for this yard at Thurles in February over hurdles (2m7f, soft) when third of 17; pulled up next time at Clonmel (3m, heavy); made chasing debut back at that venue last time in first-time cheekpieces (retained) when ninth of 14 (3m, soft); more needed.
runners.comment: Good effort over hurdles in Feb'; ninth of 14 on chasing debut latest; more needed

runners.name: Ardaghs Choice
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdles winner has one chase win as well, gained at Wexford in 2022; recent efforts don't suggest he's on the verge of adding to his tally.
runners.comment: Sole chase win came in 2022; recent runs don't suggest he's on verge of adding to tally

runners.name: Hello Johnjoe
runners.spotlight: First reserve; good effort when third in second handicap start at Clonmel (2m4f, heavy); hasn't backed that up in two starts since, including when 32l behind at this venue last time (2m5f, yielding); tries chasing now
runners.comment: Reserve; third on second h'cap start; hasn't backed that up in two runs since; chase debut

runners.name: Mastermind
runners.spotlight: Dual winning hurdler is 0-13 chasing; one of his better efforts came at Thurles in February when a 9.75l third (2m6f, soft to heavy); didn't back that up when tailed off at Limerick last time (2m7f, heavy).
runners.comment: 0-13 chasing; one of better efforts when third at Thurles but didn't back it up last time

runners.name: A Year Older
runners.spotlight: Sprang a surprise at Fairyhouse (3m2f, yielding) on New Year's Day when returning from a 294-day absence to win comfortably at 40-1 over hurdles; had shown little in his eight previous outings, including when pulled up on only start over fences; form of those in behind bolsters his win; runs off winning mark and he will win if he can back it up now chasing.
runners.comment: Surprise winner last time at 40-1; did it easily and form in behind is strong; big chance

races.race_name: 3A's Wales' Largest Motorhome Sales Mares' Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Feet On The Ground
runners.spotlight: Three seconds among her minor honours but below form on last three outings, with tongue tied on last two; a leading contender if judged on her Southwell second (extended 2m4f, good to soft) in December.
runners.comment: Three seconds among her minor honours but below form on last three outings

runners.name: Jlow
runners.spotlight: Tailed off on the Flat in September on final start last year and pulled up over hurdles last month on her only start since; place claims judged on efforts last summer but the bottom line may well be that she's 0-17.
runners.comment: Place claims judged on efforts last summer but the bottom line may well be that she's 0-17

runners.name: Yanka Blue
runners.spotlight: Second in maiden hurdle at Sedgefield (2m1f, good) in November on final start for Alison Thorpe and 19l second in a handicap at Fakenham (2m, soft) in December on her only run for the Flints; with another new yard after 112 days off, with place chance if she resumes in the same vein.
runners.comment: Another new yard after 112 days off, with place chance if she resumes in the same vein

runners.name: Camshaft
runners.spotlight: Beaten 30l, 82l and 47l over hurdles in her only three races (2m3f-2m5f); no solid form but handicapping should offer her more of a chance and she also now sports headgear; market may guide.
runners.comment: No solid form; handicapping should offer more of a chance and she also now sports headgear

runners.name: Half Pint Daisy
runners.spotlight: 4lb wrong today in her fourth handicap and she has very ropey claims; maybe a move away from soft ground will help.
runners.comment: 4lb wrong today in her fourth handicap and she has very ropey claims

runners.name: Miss Kassiopi
runners.spotlight: Back to form on last three starts (about 2m4f, variety of ground) and latest saw her get off the mark at 15th attempt, doing so by 9l from the front in a six-runner race at Hereford on Monday; carries 7lb penalty in a larger field but should have a huge shout as an in-form candidate against unpersuasive rivals.
runners.comment: Won well at Hereford on Monday; 7lb penalty but respected against unpersuasive rivals

runners.name: La Belle Argentee
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability initially over hurdles but failed to cut any ice in her two handicaps (2m and near 2m4f, soft); same owners but she's now left Dan Skelton and tries tongue-tie and cheekpieces both for first time.
runners.comment: Failed to cut any ice in two handicaps for Dan Skelton; tries tongue-tie and cheekpieces

runners.name: Lily Grace
runners.spotlight: Closest finish was a 30l defeat at Stratford (2m7f, soft; 33-1) last time in her second handicap; no recognisable claims yet.
runners.comment: Closest was a 30l defeat at Stratford (2m7f, soft; 33-1) last time in her second handicap

runners.name: Polkaface
runners.spotlight: Bits of modest form in her three bumpers and three maiden hurdles, easily best from this sphere over 2m5f at Huntingdon (heavy) on penultimate start; tongue tied last two outings; needs improvement on this handicap debut but it looks a weak race.
runners.comment: Needs improvement on this handicap debut but it looks a weak race

runners.name: Milady Du Breuil
runners.spotlight: The whiff of ability in her one bumper and three hurdle races does not get near today's opening mark but she moves up from 2m for this handicap debut; worth a market check.
runners.comment: Whiff of ability does not get near today's opening mark, but up from 2m for handicap debut

runners.name: Tipperary Star
runners.spotlight: Thrown in judged on 2022 bumpers but her four hurdle races have yielded nothing approaching that; two wind operations (2023 and 2025); makes handicap debut from 6lb wrong; Sean Bowen rides and it will probably be instructive to gauge market strength.
runners.comment: Makes handicap debut from 6lb wrong; probably instructive to gauge market strength

runners.name: Paradise Park
runners.spotlight: Fourth in her only bumper (April 2024) but beaten at least 30l in her four hurdles races, the latest when 80-1 for her handicap debut at Hereford (2m, good) five weeks ago; now has a second attempt beyond 2m.
runners.comment: Beaten at least 30l in her four hurdles races, the latest when 80-1 for her handicap debut

races.race_name: Amroth Bay Static Caravan Sales Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Doujadou
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish point in November; second to a good one at Chepstow (2m, soft) was a fine start over hurdles, which he probably didn't match when third at Newbury (2m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago, but he's still one of the leading players in this line-up.
runners.comment: Chepstow 2nd was fine start over hurdles, not matched next time but he's a leading player

runners.name: Knead A Win
runners.spotlight: Ran out on debut; however, he's been placed from the front on testing ground in his three maiden hurdles since, tongue tied first time when stepped up to 2m4f at Chepstow latest; absent since November and moving away from soft ground would ask a question, but his form suggests a big run.
runners.comment: Placed from the front on testing ground in three maiden hurdles; form suggests a big run

runners.name: Into The Blue
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam fair 2m hurdle winner, sister to high-class 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Radjhani Express, half-sister to useful 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Shatabdi, out of 2m2f Flat and useful 2m1f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner; one to note on debut for notable connections.
runners.comment: Dam winning sister to high-class 2m1f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner Radjhani Express

runners.name: Karpenn
runners.spotlight: Three minor honours in five-race French career; had wind surgery in December, before an underwhelming British debut in maiden hurdle at Exeter (2m, good to soft; beaten 31l at 10-1) in March which suggests he is best watched.
runners.comment: Three minor honours in France; had wind surgery; underwhelming British debut in March

runners.name: Guardami Ancora
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in three of five races, all over hurdles; best form on last three outings, the latest at Hereford (2m4f, good) last month, and he'll probably be placed at best again.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three of five races, all over hurdles; probably placed at best again

runners.name: Pappariche
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros 3yo; third foal; half-sister to bumper winner Hemlock; dam pulled up in a point, half-sister to winners Massini's Maguire (smart bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Twirling Magnet (useful bumper and 2m-3m hurdle/chase); first and second this year in two Irish points for mares; could be an interesting recruit, albeit perhaps more for a later stage.
runners.comment: Well related; first and second this year in two Irish points for mares

runners.name: Grand Teton
runners.spotlight: 100,000euros 3yo; half-brother to three winners, two useful; 10-1 when 12l fifth of six in novice at Sandown (2m, heavy) ten weeks ago, which does not set the world alight in form terms but he was a detached last at halfway and made late headway; moves up in trip and might be a big improver but his powerful stable also runs an eye-catching newcomer in Into The Blue.
runners.comment: Might be a big improver on this second start; powerful yard also runs eyecatching newcomer
runners.owner: M Buckley, C Noell And M Hankin

runners.name: Johnny's Belle
runners.spotlight: Started this year with first and second in two Irish points for mares; the minor honours since in a bumper here and 2m4f maiden hurdle at Hereford represent modest form, so she has major work to do.
runners.comment: First and second in two Irish points for mares; the minor honours since are modest form

runners.name: Savon
runners.spotlight: Well related for the Flat but he faded to meet with a heavy defeat in bumper at Doncaster (good to soft; 14-1) one month ago.
runners.comment: Faded to a heavy defeat in bumper at Doncaster (good to soft; 14-1) one month ago

runners.name: Shuil Rocco
runners.spotlight: Ran in two points (good) last May, third of five over 2m4f on second occasion; 26l seventh of ten in bumper at Chepstow (good to soft; 28-1) 17 days ago suggests he is a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Ran in points; 26l seventh of ten in bumper at Chepstow (good to soft; 28-1) 17 days ago

runners.name: Mr Rafiki
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to five winners including Yeah Man (2m7f hurdle/3m4f chase), Fletcher (2m3f/2m4f hurdle) and The Jukebox Kid (bumper, 2m4f-3m hurdle/chase), all useful; tongue tied and 7-1 outsider of four when he trailed in last at Ludlow (good to firm) in a bumper in October; had a wind operation soon afterwards and needs a transformation.
runners.comment: Useful pedigree; last of 4 at Ludlow (good to firm) in a bumper in October; wind op since

runners.name: Cinquenta
runners.spotlight: Has a consistent record, latterly over 2m4f with win at Market Rasen (good to soft) then respectable sixth in big novice handicap at Sandown; gives the impression he'll be as effective back down in trip; the form pick.
runners.comment: Consistent; ran respectably in big novice handicap at Sandown last time; the form pick

runners.name: My Boy Aaron
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who has shown better hurdles form, namely third-place finishes over 2m at Newbury and Exeter, since having wind surgery; again holds place claims.
runners.comment: Has shown better hurdles form since having wind surgery; again holds place claims

runners.name: Ettore
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-4 on Flat for previous yard; has lacked fluency in two attempts over hurdles (2m) but managed to run well, clear third, at Taunton latest; may take another step forward, especially if jumping better.
runners.comment: Has lacked fluency in two hurdles attempts but ran well, clear third, at Taunton latest
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late John Connolly

runners.name: Bebe And Bean
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in bumpers during the winter of 2024-25 for Paul Nicholls, finishing fifth both times; the form has substance; remains in same ownership; interesting on belated reappearance, with improvement possible.
runners.comment: Bumper form for Paul Nicholls has substance; interesting with improvement possible

runners.name: Berkshire Smudge
runners.spotlight: Has shaped with some promise, without making a major impact, in juvenile hurdles at Doncaster and Chepstow; may show improvement returned to Ffos Las, having gained sole Flat win here (soft); interesting.
runners.comment: May improve returned to Ffos Las, having gained sole Flat win here; interesting
runners.owner: Berkshire Parts & Panels Ltd No1 Fanclub

runners.name: Vivre Le Reve
runners.spotlight: Bought for £100,000 after winning sole start in France; disappointing in two juvenile hurdles in Britain, folding tamely both times, but he's in excellent hands and can't be written off yet.
runners.comment: Won sole French start; disappointing in two British attempts but can't be written off yet
runners.owner: Burns, P & M Morrall, Stokes & Wicks

runners.name: Barford Brook
runners.spotlight: Achieved nothing, eventually pulled up, in Exeter contest (2m) for an inauspicious debut.
runners.comment: Achieved nothing in Exeter contest

runners.name: Bahati Bahati
runners.spotlight: Has weak claims on point and hurdles form; handicaps are likely to be more suitable shortly.
runners.comment: Weak claims on form

runners.name: Toni's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on her Flat record and one attempt over hurdles.
runners.comment: Far from solid on form

runners.name: Angel Wave
runners.spotlight: Beaten long way in a couple of 2m3f events on soft/heavy but gives the impression he may improve for this drop in distance and switch to forecast faster ground.
runners.comment: Gives the impression he may improve for this drop in trip and switch to faster ground

runners.name: Annual Invitation
runners.spotlight: Placed in a couple of Irish points; didn't fare nearly so well in Bangor maiden hurdle (2m3f) on the switch to rules and may be one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Placed in Irish points; didn't fare nearly so well in Bangor maiden hurdle

runners.name: Laios Tresor
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in big field over 2m at Huntingdon, failing to build on his effort in small-field Irish point.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten at Huntingdon on rules debut

runners.name: Thewoodcorner
runners.spotlight: Won Chepstow novice (2m, soft) in January and kept on well for third when upped to 2m3f for last month's handicap debut at Newbury (good to soft); 1lb lower here and not yet fully exposed; must be considered.
runners.comment: Kept on well for third on last month's handicap debut at Newbury; 1lb lower here

runners.name: El Gavilan
runners.spotlight: Aided by cheekpieces this year, winning three in a row here (2m-2m4f, heavy) in January/February; also suited by faster ground but managed only fifth behind Jack's Jury off this career-high mark over C&D (soft) last month.
runners.comment: Three course wins this year; managed only fifth off this career-high mark last time

runners.name: Tradecraft
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 7yo who won a three-runner Sandown novice (2m, soft) last season; well beaten in two 2m4f handicap chases in the autumn (blinkered last time) and now reverts to hurdling in cheekpieces; tricky to weigh up.
runners.comment: Low-mileage 7yo; well beaten in two chases in the autumn; back hurdling in cheekpieces

runners.name: Jack's Jury
runners.spotlight: Back in prime form when scoring over C&D (soft; first-time cheekpieces) on last month's stable debut; not discredited when third under penalty at Chepstow (2m7f, good to soft) ten days later but others appeal more.
runners.comment: Won over C&D on last month's stable debut but this race is more competitive

runners.name: Mossy Fen Coolio
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who displayed clear promise on his first two hurdling starts, more recently when second of ten at Bangor (2m3f, good) three weeks ago; can improve in handicaps; likely contender.
runners.comment: Displayed clear promise on first two hurdle starts; likely contender on handicap debut

runners.name: Milldam
runners.spotlight: Five-time hurdle winner; inconsistent this season and not yet proven over this far but the form of his fifth in a valuable race at Windsor (2m, soft) in January reads very well in this context, and he didn't run badly when fourth at Stratford (2m, soft) last month; big player if on song.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this season but will be dangerous off current mark if on song

runners.name: Shabalko d'Herm
runners.spotlight: Launched hurdling career with two 2m wins in the autumn (good/soft) but weakened tamely on next two outings (bled from nose last time); up in trip for handicap debut and may still have potential but comes with risk.
runners.comment: Won first two hurdle races but weakened tamely on next two outings; handicap debut

runners.name: Manigod
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on first three hurdling starts before winning by over 4l on handicap debut at Ludlow (2m5f, soft) in February; 7lb higher here but will be fine on the forecast faster ground and could have more to offer.
runners.comment: Clear winner on handicap debut in February and will be fine on the faster ground here

runners.name: Meatloaf
runners.spotlight: Placed off 1lb lower at Wincanton (2m4f, soft) in January and is also suited by faster ground but weakened tamely on latest outing and has basically become a shade disappointing; sports first-time cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Placed two starts ago but weakened tamely last time; cheekpieces now added
runners.owner: The Stewart Family, Sira Ferguson & G Mason

runners.name: Watchful Protector
runners.spotlight: Won Tipperary maiden (2m3f, good to yielding) for Emmet Mullins in October but was well below that form when fifth in Chepstow novice (2m7f, good to soft) for new stable in December; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added today; needs a much better performance.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten over 2m7f on stable debut; drops back in trip with cheekpieces added

runners.name: Fresh Speculation
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season, the latter over C&D (soft); posted another career-best effort when close second to El Gavilan over C&D (heavy) in February but may not be as good on the much quicker ground today.
runners.comment: In career-best form over C&D this year but the ground is likely to be much quicker today

races.race_name: 3A's Horsebox Hire And Sales Handicap Chase

runners.name: Boston Joe
runners.spotlight: Prevailed from a solid rival in similar event over C&D (soft) last time, taking Ffos Las record to 4-15; should remain competitive despite going back up 4lb.
runners.comment: Prevailed in similar event over C&D last time, taking Ffos Las record to 4-15

runners.name: Lunar Ocean
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but is threatening to win, having finished second in six of his eight chase attempts, latest in first-time visor at Plumpton (2m4f, good); solid chance.
runners.comment: Threatening to win, having finished second in six of his eight chase attempts; solid

runners.name: L'Empire Vert
runners.spotlight: Best form over 2m on good ground, including win in autumn and second-place finish at Hereford last time; step back up in distance presents a question mark.
runners.comment: Best form over 2m on good ground; something to prove back up in trip
runners.owner: E A R Morgans, C A Pengelly, G Wilson

runners.name: Ferando
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden whose chance of getting off the mark depends on whether a change of equipment prompts improvement; cheekpieces replace usual tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Chance depends on whether change of equipment sparks improvement

runners.name: Just Aidan
runners.spotlight: Mixed results (U15) since wearing cheekpieces and switched to chasing; disappointing favourite on good ground last time; rebound needed in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Mixed results (U15) since switched to chasing; disappointing favourite last time

runners.name: Italian Spirit
runners.spotlight: Record of 0-4 since switched to fences but shaped better than bare result with blinkers fitted at Exeter (3m) most recently; drop back in trip looks ideal; could go well.
runners.comment: 0-4 over fences but shaped better than the bare result last time; could go well

runners.name: Dublin To Milan
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in both starts switched to chasing this term but can't be written off; remains the type who should do better in this sphere, having shown some promise in points.
runners.comment: Pulled up in both starts since switched to chasing but can't be written off yet

runners.name: Golden Millie
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 8yo who may improve for this combination of switch to chasing and return to Ffos Las; sole course start resulted in bumper win in 2022 (peak form).
runners.comment: May improve for the combination of switch to chasing and return to Ffos Las

runners.name: Devilsrollthedice
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has pointing experience; folded tamely at Exeter (3m) on the switch to chasing most recently but may see his race out better back down in distance.
runners.comment: May see his race out better back down in distance; second chase attempt

runners.name: Ezmerellda
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner in 2023 for previous yard; form for new stable is far from convincing; switch to fences needs to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Form for new stable is far from convincing; chase debut

runners.name: Manhattan Valley
runners.spotlight: The form of his point success has substance; unconvincing results over hurdles but looks open to improvement on this handicap/chase debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Interesting on handicap/chase debut; the form of his point success has substance

runners.name: Bridget Mary
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-7 over rules fences features a comfortable success on good ground at Bangor (3m) last April; best form this term when going very close at Chepstow (2m7f) most recently; another spring win looks plausible.
runners.comment: Won well last April; best form this term when going very close at Chepstow most recently

runners.name: Montmartin
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; respectable third (off 5lb higher) in this race last year on chase debut; hasn't fared nearly so well in two starts elsewhere this term but may stage a revival returned to this scenario with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Return to this scenario may suit; respectable third in this race last year (chase debut)

runners.name: The Magus
runners.spotlight: Suited by good ground; 0-5 since switched to fences but posted a solid effort, behind a subsequent scorer, at Hereford (3m1f) most recently; defied a 3lb higher mark last April for sole hurdles success; could well open his chase account.
runners.comment: Solid effort at Hereford last time; won off 3lb higher over hurdles last April; respected

runners.name: Mythe Bridge
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but has a broadly consistent record (223F3) since switched to chasing, albeit proved unable to justify favouritism with cheekpieces fitted at Wetherby (3m, soft) most recently; place claims in the retained headgear.
runners.comment: Maiden who has broadly consistent form (223F3) since switched to chasing; place claims

runners.name: Vision De Maine
runners.spotlight: Commenced chase career with two wins; 0-7 since and has failed to back up reappearance effort in a light current campaign; however, this combination of a drop back in class and return to forecast faster ground may prompt a resurgence.
runners.comment: Drop back in class and return to forecast faster ground may prompt a resurgence

runners.name: River Run Free
runners.spotlight: Regained the winning thread at Leicester (2m6f) in January but back on the regress since, having performed below market expectations at Ffos Las (only third of four finishers) and Leicester (ran dismally).
runners.comment: Back on the regress since Leicester win in January; ran dismally last time

runners.name: Flemenstide
runners.spotlight: Bumper/hurdles winner in 2021 for previous stable; not exactly solid on his chase form since returning from long absence and needs to build on latest effort, which was somewhat resurgent but only modest (RPR 90).
runners.comment: Not exactly solid on his chase form since returning from long absence

runners.name: Bali Body
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 11yo who isn't particularly solid, having failed to complete in three of his last four appearances; it remains a long time since his sole chase win; however, drops in grade off an attractive mark on best form.
runners.comment: Far from solid on recent form but drops in grade off an attractive mark

races.race_name: Barry Murphy Racing Syndicate Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Atlantic Power
runners.spotlight: Two wins this season, the latter at Lingfield (2m7f, heavy) in February; disappointed on latest outing and will have something to prove if the ground remains good but Sean Bowen is a good booking.
runners.comment: Two wins this season; disappointed on latest outing but Sean Bowen is a good booking today

runners.name: Pooley's Promise
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify prominence in market when well-held fifth on recent stable debut at Taunton (3m, good; first-time cheekieces) but now 2lb lower than when winning over C&D (good) for Ryan Potter last spring; has tongue-tie fitted today; interesting if strong in the betting again.
runners.comment: Well-held fifth on recent stable but won over C&D last spring; market strength interesting

runners.name: Glengolly
runners.spotlight: Seemingly aided by new tongue-tie when gaining breakthrough win at Hereford (3m1f, soft) in December, his 13th hurdle run, and has been placed on three of his four starts since; would appeal more on ground slower than good but still enters calculations; swaps usual cheekpieces for visor.
runners.comment: Placed three times since breakthrough win in December; enters calculations

runners.name: Makinbecon
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in three slow-ground maiden/novice hurdles this year (2m-2m4f; latest run was stable debut) but came second in two 3m Irish points last April (good/yielding) and looks interesting now back up in trip and back on forecast good ground for this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Well beaten in three slow-ground hurdles but may fare better on this handicap debut

runners.name: Mind Hunter
runners.spotlight: In fairly good form when last seen in the autumn and still lightly raced over this sort of trip but his only win from 18 hurdling starts was nearly three years ago; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: In fairly good form when last seen in the autumn but has modest strike-rate

runners.name: Les's Destiny Star
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; remote when fourth on handicap debut at Doncaster (3m, good to soft) in February but was given a wind op last month and remains open to improvement over hurdles for his respected stable.
runners.comment: Remote when fourth on handicap debut but has been given a wind op since; still unexposed

runners.name: Bradman
runners.spotlight: Struck form with two 2m3f wins at Fontwell (good to soft/soft) this winter, and may not have been suited by heavy ground when disappointing on latest outing (2m4f); could have a say if suited by this new trip.
runners.comment: Two 2m3f wins this winter; had ground excuse last time; upped in trip today

runners.name: Getaway Sam
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles, including four non-completions; responded well to first-time cheekpieces when close second at Warwick (3m2f, good) last month but that was a modest race; others here are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Close second in new cheekpieces at Warwick last month but that was a modest race

runners.name: Kantagua Du Large
runners.spotlight: Ex-French mare; generally disappointing in Britain since start of 2025 but looked suited by step up to 3m when respectable fourth off much reduced mark at Taunton (good) last month; a possible.
runners.comment: 0-10 over hurdles but looked suited by step up to 3m when fourth at Taunton last month

runners.name: An Buachaill Rua
runners.spotlight: Came good at Ludlow (2m5f, good to soft) in February, his seventh hurdle run, and probably bettered that performance when close second at Bangor (2m7f, good) last month; still unexposed as a stayer; respected.
runners.comment: Won over 2m5f in February then showed better form when close second over 2m7f last month

runners.name: Miss Jaffa Cake
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in her first two handicaps (2m6f/3m2f) and competes from 5lb out of the weights here; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in her first two handicaps and competes from 5lb out of the weights here

runners.name: Cote d'Ange
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only four runs but was just a respectable third of seven when upped from 2m to 2m5f for handicap debut at Hereford (soft) in February and needs improvement here.
runners.comment: Just a respectable third of seven on handicap debut (2m5f) and needs improvement here

runners.name: Kingston Kid
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on old Irish form but lightly raced and disappointing in recent years; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old Irish form but lightly raced and disappointing in recent years

runners.name: Star Turn
runners.spotlight: 0-11 under all codes and absent since pulled up in 2m2f chase in February 2025; given a chance by the handicapper but could be considered only if lively in the betting; first run beyond about 2m3f; the visor is new.
runners.comment: 0-11 under all codes and absent since pulled up in 2m2f chase in February 2025

runners.name: I Am Spider Man
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on heavy ground for Debra Hamer in January but wasn't beaten far on first run for that stable, at Taunton (3m, good) in December; now with another new trainer and has become very well handicapped on old form; needs close attention in the betting.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on old form and needs close attention in the betting on stable debut

runners.name: Rock On Cassie
runners.spotlight: Pulled up on three of her four starts since returning from a mammoth absence this season, and has a lot to prove on first venture beyond 2m6f under rules.
runners.comment: Pulled up on three of her four starts since returning from a mammoth absence this season

runners.name: Ka Ying Grateful
runners.spotlight: Out of a dual 1m winner; seemed to take a step in the right direction in his latest trial but probably needs to make further progress on this official debut; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Latest trial was a step in the right direction but he needs further progress today

runners.name: Perfect One
runners.spotlight: Bred to be better suited by much longer trips than this but came second in a 5f trial last month and the booking of Zac Purton suggests a big run is expected.
runners.comment: Came second in a 5f trial last month and Zac Purton rides here; likely contender

runners.name: Silvery Knight
runners.spotlight: Out of a three-time winner (6.5f-1m) who has produced a German 2,000gns winner; makes official debut after some inauspicious trials.
runners.comment: Makes official debut after some inauspicious trials

runners.name: Glorious Hero
runners.spotlight: Out of a 1m winner; appeared to have a bit still to learn when down the field in his two trials.
runners.comment: Appeared to have a bit still to learn when down the field in his two trials

runners.name: Secret Ingredient
runners.spotlight: Looked more professional than previously when third in a recent 4f trial but still comes with risk attached on official debut.
runners.comment: Latest trial was more encouraging but he sill comes with risk attached on debut

runners.name: Talents Champion
runners.spotlight: Out of a dual Group 3 sprint winner; made steady progress in his trials and wasn't beaten far when fourth over 4f in the most recent; one to consider.
runners.comment: Out of a dual Group 3 sprint winner; made steady progress in his trials; considered

runners.name: Yee Cheong Raider
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden but deserves a break and had some of these behind (Dash was third) when beating all bar the favourite over 6f here last month; would have been interesting returned to 7f but for stall 12.
runners.comment: Would have been interesting returned to 7f but for stall 12
runners.owner: Choi Hiu Fung & Choi Chun Lung

runners.name: Winning Machine
runners.spotlight: Excellent second from a bad draw over C&D two runs back and that was his yard debut; no excuses from a good position when fourth two weeks ago but he's got an ideal draw here and Moreira takes over.
runners.comment: Running fairly well of late; he's got an ideal draw and Moreira takes over
runners.owner: Sammy Ma Hon Kit/Chan Sin Shui/Exec Of Est Of J C T Cheung

runners.name: Giddy Up
runners.spotlight: Reserve; hard to win with at the best of times and he looks on a downer at the minute.
runners.comment: Reserve; hard to win with at the best of times and he looks on a downer at the minute

runners.name: Panda Legend
runners.spotlight: Only 1-21 but continues to run well now and again and this is his grade; however, stall 13 is offputting.
runners.comment: Continues to run well now and again and this is his grade; however, stall 13 is offputting

runners.name: Island Golden
runners.spotlight: Poor strike-rate and both wins have been over shorter; doesn't arrive with any recent form on the board.
runners.comment: Both wins over 6f and doesn't arrive with any recent form on the board

runners.name: Dash
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden but appreciated the drop to this level when a running-on third over 6f here four weeks ago and he's since won a trial; quite interesting now raised a furlong from a good draw.
runners.comment: A running-on third over 6f here four weeks ago and he's since won a trial

runners.name: Good Fortune

runners.comment: Last month's fifths here were close ones and he's now dropped into a Class 5

runners.comment: Happy Valley regular; has become very hard to win with, his losing run now 23

runners.name: Basic Instinct

runners.comment: Well beaten dropped into a Class 5 on last official run and has stamina to prove
runners.owner: 22/23 Me Tsui Yu Sak Trainer Syndicate

runners.name: He Was Me

runners.comment: C&D winner last month; only eighth on his return visit and was behind Winning Machine

runners.name: Mr Good Vibes

runners.comment: 1m winner here in Nov; subsequent defeats have been over 6f, can do better over this trip

runners.name: One Love

runners.comment: Very opposable on his form in Hong Kong and only fourth in a trial since last official run

runners.name: Call To Command
runners.spotlight: No wins in 15 but was only a length away in a Class 5 at Happy Valley two runs back (over 6f) and was poorly drawn in a Class 4 back there last time; probably has a race in him off this mark and gives the impression that he'll stay 7f.
runners.comment: Probably has a race in him off this mark and gives the impression that he'll stay 7f

runners.name: Winning Diamond
runners.spotlight: Another runner who has been contesting higher-grade races but hasn't been showing a lot, from 5f to 1m.
runners.comment: Another runner who has been contesting higher-grade races but hasn't been showing a lot

runners.name: Winning Cigar
runners.spotlight: Disappointing dropped into a Class 5 on last official run and has since finished last in a trial; now 0-8.
runners.comment: Disappointing dropped into a Class 5 on last run and has since finished last in a trial

runners.name: View All Things
runners.spotlight: Reserve; no placed form in 11 runs and best trip remains open to debate; only fifth over 6f here last week.
runners.comment: Reserve; no placed form in 11 runs and best trip remains open to debate

runners.name: Mr Aladdin
runners.spotlight: Placed a few times but a longstanding maiden and hasn't threatened for a while; out of the weights.
runners.comment: Placed a few times but a longstanding maiden and hasn't threatened for a while

runners.name: Harry's Hero
runners.spotlight: Another with a poor strike-rate but twice went agonisingly close over 1m here and at Happy Valley before struggling twice from wicked draws; now in box 4, he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Suffered two near misses before struggling from wicked draws on last two starts

runners.name: The Way We Win
runners.spotlight: No wins in 20 and carrying more weight than his official rating entitles him to.
runners.comment: No wins in 20 and carrying more weight than his official rating entitles him to

runners.name: Windicator Family
runners.spotlight: Behind some of these here last time but over 7f and this is more his trip; however, a strike-rate of 1-30 speaks volumes.
runners.comment: Behind some of these here last time but over 7f and this is more his trip; only 1-30
runners.owner: Thomas Poon Ho Wing & Poon Chi Keung

runners.name: Gold Tack
runners.spotlight: 1-36, the win coming over 1m1f here last summer; back on a competitive mark but hasn't raced officially for three months and recent trial runs were uninspiring.
runners.comment: Hasn't raced officially for three months and recent trial runs were uninspiring
runners.owner: Robert Chung Wai Shu & Jeanette Chung Siu Wah

runners.name: Smiling Emperor
runners.spotlight: Reserve; won at Happy Valley last summer but hasn't been threatening to double his tally.
runners.comment: Reserve; won at Happy Valley last summer but hasn't been threatening to double his tally

runners.name: Soaring Bronco
runners.spotlight: Reserve; C&D winner and first run for this yard when dead-heating at Happy Valley last month (1m); overcame a troubled run off 5lb lower so good chance if he gets in.
runners.comment: Reserve; C&D winner and first run for this yard when dead-heating at Happy Valley
runners.owner: Simon Yung Yun Sang, Tang Ka Nin Et Al

runners.name: Casa Legend
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner and was only 2l away here a month ago over 7f after losing his position mid-race; should stand some sort of chance.
runners.comment: Only 2l away here a month ago over 7f after losing his position mid-race; better at 1m

runners.name: Speedy Trident
runners.spotlight: Opposable on a few counts as he's still a maiden, isn't in great form and has stamina to prove.
runners.comment: Opposable on a few counts; still a maiden, isn't in great form and has stamina to prove

runners.name: Beauty Missile

runners.comment: Strike-rate of 1-38 but that win came recently and his last run was a good one

runners.name: Rattan Galaxy
runners.spotlight: His trial win was over 6f and the near miss here in December over 7f; didn't convince with his stamina over 1m1f here last time and even this far could be a stretch.
runners.comment: Didn't convince with his stamina over 1m1f last time and even this far could be a stretch

runners.name: Bling Bling Genius
runners.spotlight: His previous attempt at 1m was too bad to be true and he has since produced two good efforts over 7f at this track, beating all bar the favourite two weeks ago; the burning question is whether he'll get the trip.
runners.comment: Excellent runs over 7f here the last twice; burning question is whether he'll get the trip

runners.name: General Smart
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in a recent trial under Zac Purton but the top rider was aboard for his narrow miss over 7f here last month; however, has finished nowhere in two previous attempts at 1m and he's drawn wide.
runners.comment: Has finished nowhere in two previous attempts at 1m and he's drawn wide
runners.owner: Alex Chau Man Lok, Jack Chan Hoi Et Al

runners.name: Dashing Maurison
runners.spotlight: Struggled from a bad draw last time but had been knocking on the door and this is his first time in a Class 5 handicap; third in a recent trial; possibilities.
runners.comment: Has been knocking on the door and this is his first time in a Class 5 handicap

runners.name: Iron Legion
runners.spotlight: Regular at this level; narrow winner from Harry's Hero over C&D in January but lesser runs have followed, and beaten in two trials last month.
runners.comment: Narrow winner from Harry's Hero over C&D in January but lesser runs have followed
runners.owner: The Hon & Mrs Kenneth Lau Ip Keung

runners.name: Cool Blue
runners.spotlight: Ridden by Zac Purton on last two appearances and finished well to seal the deal over 1m1f here in February; 6lb higher now and Purton looks elsewhere.
runners.comment: Recent course winner over 1m1f; 6lb higher now and Purton looks elsewhere

runners.name: Amazing Fun
runners.spotlight: Near miss off a reduced mark over C&D in January but two very disappointing runs have followed.
runners.comment: Suffered a near miss here in January but two very disappointing runs have followed

runners.name: Sweet Briar
runners.spotlight: Has dropped to a very appealing mark but seemingly for good reason; opposable right now.
runners.comment: Has dropped to a very appealing mark but seemingly for good reason; opposable right now
runners.owner: Sammy To Ping Kwan & Cheng Shenn

runners.name: Eighty Light Years
runners.spotlight: Only 2-32 but couldn't have gone closer from an inside stall here three weeks ago; drawn in 10 this time and perhaps vulnerable.
runners.comment: Only 2-32 but couldn't have gone closer from an inside stall here three weeks ago

runners.name: Happy Boss
runners.spotlight: C&D winner on his second career start in December and might well have gone in again here four weeks ago with a clearer run; however, that was from stall 2 and now he's in 13; might do well to overcome that.
runners.comment: Unlucky here last time but that was from stall 2 and now he's in 13

runners.name: The Concentration
runners.spotlight: Has been plying his trade in Class 5s but he only narrowly failed to bring up the hat-trick last time; raised 2lb for that but firmly in the reckoning over his optimum trip.
runners.comment: Upgraded here but he's been bang in form in Class 5s; nearly won his last race as well

runners.name: Gallant Design
runners.spotlight: Unlucky not to make winning debut over 6f here in January and again didn't get the breaks last time over 7f, when 2l off the winner after starting slowly (Eighty Light Years runner-up); could be better drawn but his turn looks near.
runners.comment: Positives to take from all three runs; lightly raced and his turn looks near

runners.name: Invictus Dragon
runners.spotlight: Yet to win but hasn't always had luck on his side and ran a cracker to be second to an in-form rival from stall 12 over C&D two weeks ago; Zac Purton keeps the faith.
runners.comment: Ran a cracker to be second to an in-form rival from stall 12 over C&D two weeks ago

runners.name: Top Time
runners.spotlight: A winner over further at Happy Valley 12 months ago; had been running pretty well over 7f/1m before being drawn out of contention last time; that was two months ago but he has since finished second in a trial.
runners.comment: In good form until drawn out of contention on final run; second in a recent trial

runners.name: Supreme Feeling
runners.spotlight: Still early doors but has yet to finish closer than seventh and may need further help from the handicapper; all runs over 6f.
runners.comment: Has yet to finish closer than seventh and may need further help from the handicapper
runners.owner: Dr & Mrs Anthony W K Chow & Justin Chow

runners.name: Cheerful Wongchoy
runners.spotlight: Belated debut when shaping okay at Happy Valley but his second run there not so encouraging; steps up to 7f from 6f.
runners.comment: Debut was okay but second run at Happy Valley not so encouraging

runners.name: Golden Win
runners.spotlight: Smidgens of promise in two of his three races over 6f at this track; mark has dropped only 4lb but he is raised in trip and has a handy draw; possible.
runners.comment: Smidgens of promise in two of his three races over 6f at this track
runners.owner: Gibson Lau Wai Chi, Wu Kai Char Et Al

runners.name: Lightness Of Being
runners.spotlight: Won in Ireland but it's been tough work in Hong Kong and dropping in grade did nothing last time.
runners.comment: Won in Ireland but it's been tough work in Hong Kong

runners.name: Tycoon Express
runners.spotlight: Has run well in both visits here (6f/7f) as he was poorly drawn on both occasions; his luck in that respect hasn't improved but he's very much one to keep an eye on.
runners.comment: Quite eyecatching from poor draws in 6f/7f races here; unfortunately he has another
runners.owner: Brian Leung Hung Tak & Sophie Leung Lau Yau Fun

runners.name: Polar Patch
